name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: The Bills won by 1 point — let’s match it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
A narrow win in Buffalo shows us: Every point matters. Every dollar counts.
Last Sunday, the Buffalo Bills pulled off an incredible comeback — down by 15 points in the fourth quarter, they fought until the final seconds and won by just one point. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101034/569189294/246949951?amount=1&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.12_Buffalo-Bills_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGY4NDEwMS1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-9T3IiFKUw2SqaSX5zUR5WngxW2Ey_kLus70Lt4ulv8=) Jonathan and I watched in our Bills gear as they fought until the very last play. **That nail-biting finish reminded me of what it takes to win when the odds are stacked against you:_grit, determination and a team that refuses to give up._** That’s exactly what it will take to flip the Senate in 2026. Republicans have billionaire donors — and we have you. **So here’s my challenge:[**_Will you chip in $1 for every point Buffalo won by to help Democrats fight back and flip the Senate?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101036/569189296/246949951?amount=1&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.12_Buffalo-Bills_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGY4NDEwMS1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-9T3IiFKUw2SqaSX5zUR5WngxW2Ey_kLus70Lt4ulv8=)** | | [$1 FOR THE ONE-POINT WIN >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101037/569189298/246949951?amount=1&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.12_Buffalo-Bills_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGY4NDEwMS1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-9T3IiFKUw2SqaSX5zUR5WngxW2Ey_kLus70Lt4ulv8=)
---
Here’s the truth: Republicans are already flooding key states with dark money and attack ads. They’re betting we won’t have the resources to fight back.
But just like the Bills didn’t give up on Sunday, we’re not backing down. Every dollar you give goes straight to the fight: protecting Social Security, defending reproductive rights, expanding access to health care and stopping Donald Trump’s far-right agenda.
[**_So will you join us? Help us bring that same comeback energy to the fight for the Senate and pitch in $1:_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101039/569189299/246949951?amount=1&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.12_Buffalo-Bills_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGY4NDEwMS1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-9T3IiFKUw2SqaSX5zUR5WngxW2Ey_kLus70Lt4ulv8=)
| [$1 FOR THE ONE-POINT WIN >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101040/569189301/246949951?amount=1&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.12_Buffalo-Bills_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGY4NDEwMS1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-9T3IiFKUw2SqaSX5zUR5WngxW2Ey_kLus70Lt4ulv8=)

Thank you for being part of this team,
Kirsten Gillibrand

clean_body: A narrow win in Buffalo shows us: Every point matters. Every dollar counts. Last Sunday, the Buffalo Bills pulled off an incredible comeback — down by 15 points in the fourth quarter, they fought until the final seconds and won by just one point. = Jonathan and I watched in our Bills gear as they fought until the very last play. That nail-biting finish reminded me of what it takes to win when the odds are stacked against you:grit, determination and a team that refuses to give up. That’s exactly what it will take to flip the Senate in 2026. Republicans have billionaire donors — and we have you. So here’s my challenge:= | | = Here’s the truth: Republicans are already flooding key states with dark money and attack ads. They’re betting we won’t have the resources to fight back. But just like the Bills didn’t give up on Sunday, we’re not backing down. Every dollar you give goes straight to the fight: protecting Social Security, defending reproductive rights, expanding access to health care and stopping Donald Trump’s far-right agenda. = | = Thank you for being part of this team, Kirsten Gillibrand * =

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I’ll never forget the phone call I got 14 years ago
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
The day Gabby was shot
#

I’ll never forget the phone call I got 14 years ago, when Gabby was shot by someone trying to assassinate her. It was the worst day of our lives.
Today, I’m thinking of Charlie Kirk’s wife, Erika, and his two children. Their kids are going to grow up without a father. It’s a horrible, horrible thing.
In a democracy, no one should ever fear violence in response to political disagreement. No parent, spouse, or child should ever have to face this situation, but these acts of political violence have become terribly common in our country. Leaders on both ends of the political spectrum have been shot and attacked.
I worry every single day that we’ve become too divided to figure out how to stop things like this from happening. That thought weighs heavily on me.
Political rhetoric has gotten way out of hand, and anybody can see that. And weak gun laws give hateful people easy access to powerful weapons. The question before us is what we can do about it.
This has to end. It’s up to us, to me as a United States Senator, to set an example and work to do that. We have to convince people that we’re one nation, and we’re stronger when we work together. We have to recognize that, no matter how intense our disagreements, we’re all Americans. We owe that to our families, our neighbors, and ourselves.
Here’s something that gives me hope: After Gabby was shot, she did not give up. She doesn’t quit — ever. She’s worked for 14 years to stop the gun violence that almost killed her. She’s my hero, and she’s an example to all of us.
We can’t keep meeting hate with hate, violence with violence. There has to be another way.
I love this country, I believe in our democracy, and I am going to do all I can to ensure both of them endure for our children and grandchildren.
Thank you. Please, take care of yourselves.
Mark

**[MarkKelly.com](https://markkelly.com/?refcode=)**

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=6187%2E6688874%2EWnl6yh).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

I was preparing to speak in favor of a bipartisan bill that supports childcare, housing, and healthcare for all those who serve in our Armed Forces and their families. But then a person who represents South Carolina introduced several amendments that serve no purpose other than attacking and dehumanizing trans people. This person introduced those amendments using disgusting, hateful, violent language that I will not repeat. _[**So, when it was my turn to speak, I addressed that person from South Carolina* and their unhinged vendetta against our trans neighbors.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101123/569189643/-1241486548?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.09.12_FloorSpeech_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmRjMThhMy1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AIOLIKRV9b-etxACpn8qUXL7j1GfrBTlqjXHOLbB3Ig=&emci=3d498633-e78f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=ebdc18a3-fc8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=23483442)_ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101125/569189645/-2122446049?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.09.12_FloorSpeech_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmRjMThhMy1mYzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AIOLIKRV9b-etxACpn8qUXL7j1GfrBTlqjXHOLbB3Ig=&emci=3d498633-e78f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=ebdc18a3-fc8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=23483442) (*In my speech, I mistakenly said this person was from “North Carolina.” I apologize – I don’t believe in referring to someone in a way that ignores their identity.) name: Crow HQ
subject: Trump’s “Golden Age” is screwing over people in your community
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
On the campaign trail, Trump promised a new Golden Age for America.

Now? Our economy is getting weaker. Unemployment is rising. There are more unemployed people than there are available jobs. Everyday goods are too expensive for working people. And just yesterday we learned that Trump's reckless tariffs are driving up inflation while working people pay the price.

Trump’s “Golden Age” is screwing over hardworking people.

Jason is working in the House to fight back against Trump’s disastrous agenda.

He will never stop fighting for working people, because he’s from a working-class family and knows what it’s like to put in long hours to make ends meet.

Growing up, Jason worked in construction to put himself through school, served fries at Arby’s and McDonald’s, and did what he could to support his family.

Jason’s experience laid the foundation for his work today on the House floor. That’s why he’s working to elect a new generation of Democratic leaders who understand how hard everyday folks work and will fight just as hard for them in Washington. Will you pitch in right now to power Jason’s work? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

DONATE $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=10&express_lane=true]
DONATE $25 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=25&express_lane=true]

DONATE $50 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=50&express_lane=true]
DONATE $100 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=100&express_lane=true]

DONATE $250 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25?amount=250&express_lane=true]
ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25]

We are strongest when our middle and working classes are thriving. We must fight for policies that will strengthen and support middle and working-class families in every corner of the country.

Thanks for your support today.

Crow HQ
Jason Crow for Congress
8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543
Greenwood Village, CO 80112
United States
www.jasoncrowforcongress.com [https://www.jasoncrowforcongress.com/] | info@jasoncrowforcongress.com [info@jasoncrowforcongress.com]
Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress

Abigail feels the immense responsibility to deliver for Democrats at the ballot box.
That's why she traveled all across Virginia to hear directly from voters on what should be prioritized in this election and has announced plans to secure our future:
* The _Affordable Virginia Plan_ would lower the cost of healthcare, housing, and utility bills;
* The _Growing Virginia Plan_ would create opportunities through job creation, trade, and business investment;
* The _Strengthening Virginia Schools Plan_ would modernize K-12 schools, provide parents access to high-quality child care, and prepare our kids to succeed in 21st-century jobs;

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115101617/569190600/-1143777810?contactdata=hx3SOHSQ1834gDm7b4WMt2uEF06BPH2rmgQHFkMCPOmD/FQJTvAblhb/1FrGODFAJXR8nj3sqHDr/HG7MegpCsoQCSKCo1GzkGJHNCBdAPSe3u6wQXhXtWsAwFraMjIV&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTdlY2NhZDEtZmQ4Zi1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sxrRQgqG8j57UgXMuWtXQoLhFlckA4oOM5KY6UmpxLM=&emci=3b2da90f-f08f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e7eccad1-fd8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=8470899)

subject: Restore our neighbors’ Social Security numbers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sanchez
body:
In one of the most outrageous abuses of government power we’ve seen, the Trump administration falsely declared 6,300 legally present immigrants dead, and then quietly flagged their Social Security numbers as “unverified.”

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115102051/569191875/-107464461?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2025-09-12_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDllNjZhZC1mZThmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CcftiV6LXbgEPWpWvISrCn3vXOjN_eApl4oZDaeo3-s=)

| Peter, in one of the most outrageous abuses of government power we’ve seen, the Trump administration**falsely declared 6,300 legally present immigrants dead** , and then quietly flagged their Social Security numbers as _“unverified.”_

Their goal is to intimidate them into self-deporting.

These are our neighbors. Our coworkers. Parents raising their children. And now, they could be locked out of basic life necessities. This action could bar them from receiving federal benefits, working, or opening a bank account, and could even cut their citizen children off from survivor benefits in the event of their death.

I’m calling on the Trump administration to **restore these wrongfully flagged Social Security numbers immediately.**

This is a targeted attack.

[_**Will you sign my petition to demand that the Trump administration stop this gross abuse of power and restore the rights of legal immigrants?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115102052/569191876/-1017426444?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDllNjZhZC1mZThmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CcftiV6LXbgEPWpWvISrCn3vXOjN_eApl4oZDaeo3-s=&emci=82f0e0e2-168f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=409e66ad-fe8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=6681226)

name: Team Jon Ossoff
subject: Please give us a chance to explain
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
In just a moment, we’re going to ask you to make your first contribution to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign here in Georgia.
But first, we believe that you deserve an explanation for why we’re asking, particularly right now.
Here it is, Peter:
We are very quickly approaching the end of September, and with it our official FEC fundraising deadline.
After the deadline, we’re _legally required_ to file a report with not just how much money we’ve raised, but the number of donations we’ve received. And shortly afterwards, the report will be made public.
Everyone, from our opponents to Donald Trump to political pundits are going to be looking very closely to see if our campaign has what it takes to defend this seat.
We can’t risk falling short, especially right when the race is heating up. We _have_ to put up a strong showing before this critical deadline.
**So please, Peter:**
**[Give $5 — or whatever feels right — to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign today. We’re looking for lots and lots of donations before the end of this month.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-sep25.SYBMcx&refcode=em250912-1-full&t=1&akid=758%2E5691347%2EucU5MT)

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Peter, I demanded answers from RFK Jr. Here’s what happened.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
RFK Jr. is a danger to our nation’s public health. And I’m not afraid to call him out.
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T583efd28-ca91-4d57-bab0-f03f7ce97946/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter, this is Maggie Hassan. You may have seen me demand answers from RFK Jr. on his harmful anti-vaccine agenda in the Senate Finance Committee hearing recently. You may have even caught me on MSNBC or CNN the other day. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf05fdc77-6691-474f-9ffd-add0fbb3ebb5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) In case you missed it, here’s everything you need to know. RFK Jr., the Secretary of Health and Human Services under Donald Trump, continues to: ➤ Reject the vaccines that help eradicate diseases in the U.S.
➤ Ignore advice from scientists and trusted public health data.
➤ Deny that the GOP’s “One Big Beautiful Bill” cut $1 trillion from Medicaid.

[When I laid out the facts, he said I was “making stuff up.” Will you please split $10 between me and Blue Amp Action to show RFK Jr. that we won’t back down?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7307cacf-4907-4b56-965d-120e3e4a020b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| Put simply, RFK Jr. is a danger to our nation’s public health. And I’m not afraid to call him out. I’ve had enough of his dangerous actions that are putting children, families, and folks with disabilities at risk. I’ll keep holding him accountable at every opportunity I get, but I need your urgent support: [Peter, will you please split $10 or more between me and Blue Amp Action to help continue to stand up to RFK Jr.’s attacks and elect more public health champions in the midterms?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1269ba1a-3eb1-405b-b739-b18157007012/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7d93a553-da1f-44e6-88ba-13129b994c55/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

The crucial special election for Congress in Arizona’s 7th district will be decided in just 11 days, and with early voting underway, the window to make a difference is closing _fast._ **Our JanePAC-endorsed climate champion Adelita Grijalva is fighting to keep this seat out of Republican hands, and the stakes go far beyond any one seat. This is our first big test of the fall ahead of the 2026 midterms, which means everyone will be watching — the pundits, pollsters, and yes, even Big Oil, to see the strength of this team.** **[So we can’t afford to falter. Special elections are never easy. They often fly under the radar and surprise voters who didn't know they were supposed to vote, resulting in dramatically lower turnout than regular elections.
That makes every door knock, every phone call, and every dollar raised absolutely critical. This race could literally be decided by just a handful of votes, which is why your support is so crucial in these final days.
If that wasn't enough to convince you, consider this: **Adelita has a chance to continue the legacy of her late father, Congressman Raúl Grijalva, and make history as the first Latina ever elected to Congress from Arizona.**
She’s already proven as Pima County Supervisor that she can take on big polluters and win. She blocked a petrochemical plant near Tucson, protected thousands of acres of open space, and stood up for tribal sovereignty. Now, she's ready to take that same fight to Congress.
But Adelita’s MAGA opponent is pouring money into TV ads in the final days of this race, and we can’t afford to let his message go unanswered. **This is exactly when grassroots power becomes the difference between victory and defeat.**
**[Your donation doesn’t just help us reach climate voters in Arizona — it powers JanePAC’s work to elect climate champions all across the country. Can we count on you to chip in what you can today to help us fight back against the fossil fuel industry's influence?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/115102484/569193236/2105508926?refcode=ea.ee754289-ff8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.ee754289-ff8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImVlNzU0Mjg5LWZmOGYtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=fdDDyA4lOcdwIcqthjzYK1FmxSA9e0WW7SECl94jZUs=)**

clean_body: Adelita Grijalva could become Arizona’s first Latina in Congress. | = This is it. The crucial special election for Congress in Arizona’s 7th district will be decided in just 11 days, and with early voting underway, the window to make a difference is closing fast. Our JanePAC-endorsed climate champion Adelita Grijalva is fighting to keep this seat out of Republican hands, and the stakes go far beyond any one seat. This is our first big test of the fall ahead of the 2026 midterms, which means everyone will be watching — the pundits, pollsters, and yes, even Big Oil, to see the strength of this team. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Special elections are never easy. They often fly under the radar and surprise voters who didn't know they were supposed to vote, resulting in dramatically lower turnout than regular elections. That makes every door knock, every phone call, and every dollar raised absolutely critical. This race could literally be decided by just a handful of votes, which is why your support is so crucial in these final days. If that wasn't enough to convince you, consider this: Adelita has a chance to continue the legacy of her late father, Congressman Raúl Grijalva, and make history as the first Latina ever elected to Congress from Arizona. She’s already proven as Pima County Supervisor that she can take on big polluters and win. She blocked a petrochemical plant near Tucson, protected thousands of acres of open space, and stood up for tribal sovereignty. Now, she's ready to take that same fight to Congress. But Adelita’s MAGA opponent is pouring money into TV ads in the final days of this race, and we can’t afford to let his message go unanswered. This is exactly when grassroots power becomes the difference between victory and defeat. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Let’s make history together, – JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

name: Yassamin Ansari
subject: These conditions are inhumane
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
This is the reality of the war on immigrant communities. |
---
I wanted to make sure you saw my message, Peter. **This** is the reality of Trump’s war on immigrant communities: inhumane conditions for people who have not been convicted of a crime. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115102718/569194251/1289792659?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250912_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYWU3NGFlOS0wMDkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GipnQT6vBf1nspZdX_KFD6LMA5OLantPjb3mnV-ScD0=) **I won’t stop sounding the alarm — if you’re with me in this work, will you pitch in today?** **Here’s the best link to make a contribution:[secure.actblue.com/donate/ya-em-sep25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115102719/569194252/2112851217?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250912_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYWU3NGFlOS0wMDkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GipnQT6vBf1nspZdX_KFD6LMA5OLantPjb3mnV-ScD0=)** Thanks, Yassamin \---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Yassamin Ansari
Date: Saturday, Sep 6, 2025
Subject: The conditions inside ICE facilities are horrific Peter, Last weekend, I visited with the detainees being held at ICE’s Eloy Detention Center. **The conditions were horrific.** I spoke with dozens of people who came to this country seeking asylum and looked to the U.S. as a place of refuge. A woman who is fighting leukemia hasn’t had proper access to the medical treatment she requires, and is coughing up blood and has lost over 50 pounds due to the medical neglect. The people within the detention center don’t have access to quality water — the water they’ve been given has chlorine in it and is causing reactions all over their bodies. They told me of the used underwear they’re forced to wear, causing rampant UTIs. They’ve told me of the racist and retaliatory treatment they’ve received, just for speaking with me on these oversight visits. **These conditions are dehumanizing and are a stain on our society. I refuse to let this continue, but I need your support in this fight. clean_body: This is the reality of the war on immigrant communities. | I wanted to make sure you saw my message, Peter. This is the reality of Trump’s war on immigrant communities: inhumane conditions for people who have not been convicted of a crime. = I won’t stop sounding the alarm — if you’re with me in this work, will you pitch in today? Here’s the best link to make a contribution:= Thanks, Yassamin \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Yassamin Ansari Date: Saturday, Sep 6, 2025 Subject: The conditions inside ICE facilities are horrific Peter, Last weekend, I visited with the detainees being held at ICE’s Eloy Detention Center. The conditions were horrific. I spoke with dozens of people who came to this country seeking asylum and looked to the U.S. as a place of refuge. A woman who is fighting leukemia hasn’t had proper access to the medical treatment she requires, and is coughing up blood and has lost over 50 pounds due to the medical neglect. The people within the detention center don’t have access to quality water — the water they’ve been given has chlorine in it and is causing reactions all over their bodies. They told me of the used underwear they’re forced to wear, causing rampant UTIs. They’ve told me of the racist and retaliatory treatment they’ve received, just for speaking with me on these oversight visits. These conditions are dehumanizing and are a stain on our society. I refuse to let this continue, but I need your support in this fight. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Oversight is a part of my job that I don’t take lightly, Peter. We must uphold due process of law and stand up against human rights abuses. Thank you for standing with me as we demand change and call for this administration to respect the dignity of detainees. — Yassamin Yassamin Ansari for Congress PO Box 13524 Phoenix, AZ 85002 United States | mailto:info@yassaminforcongress.com | Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress.

name: Team AOC
subject: The Wizard of Oz
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
You yank back the curtain, and it's a little guy.
#
This administration is much more prepared and planned this time around, in terms of the kinds of chaos that they're trying to unleash.
More than legal programs, more than policy, **they are trying to invoke _fear_**.
That is how authoritarianism works. These are the tools to get us to sacrifice and surrender our civil liberties, more than they take them.
Trump pardoned virtually all the January 6th insurrectionists, including specifically people who were arrested with threats against my life.
**I'm not going to give them my fear**. I feel more like squaring up than anything else.
When I was running in 2018, people would say, “Why should we vote for a waitress instead of this 20-year incumbent?” — understandably, right? I didn't have experience. They did. And I often talked about the story of _The Wizard of Oz_.
This was my mom's favorite movie. I know it's in the zeitgeist now because _Wicked_ is out, but genuinely: all of these big, scary things, it reminds me of the end of _The Wizard of Oz_ , where you yank back the curtain — and _it's a little guy_.
**It's a small, scared, incompetent little man**.
I think we need to remain focused. Yes, they are capable of immense amounts of damage.
It can be scary. It's not something we ignore. It's not something we check out from.
But we register it. We say, “OK.” **Then we figure out what our options are and we do the best we can, day by day**.
They're relying on us giving up. **Hell no**.
We’re going to keep standing with trans people.
We’re going to keep standing with the LGBT community.
We’re going to keep standing for the working class.
We’re going to keep fighting for a better minimum wage.
We’re going to keep fighting to guarantee healthcare.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250912.cKtDi4&refcode=em20250912-9741-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250912.cKtDi4&refcode=em20250912-9741-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250912?refcode=em20250912-9741-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=9741_2526796_1UMuEC&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250912-9741-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=26&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)
---|---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [¿Hablas español?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/espanol-2025/?source=em20250912-9741-P_FND_EGR_GRM)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=9741%2E2526796%2E1UMuEC)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

name: Tobias Read
subject: Join me in standing up to the Trump Administration’s illegal attempts to access our voter registration data >>
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
The Trump administration just tried to force Oregon to hand over sensitive voter registration data — including information that could be misused to build a national voter database, suppress votes, or spread dangerous lies about “fraud.”
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
I said no, loud and clear. Oregon will not cave to an unlawful and unconstitutional demand.
But let’s be honest: this isn’t the last attempt. If President Trump and his allies are willing to weaponize the Department of Justice to grab private data, they won’t stop there.
That’s why I need your help. Defending our elections — and protecting your right to vote — requires everyone's involvement. My team is pushing back aggressively against federal overreach. This means educating voters, disseminating our message, and supporting pro-democracy candidates. Will you chip in $15, $25, or even $50 today to help us protect Oregon voters and safeguard our democracy? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
Every dollar you contribute will strengthen our fight to keep elections free from federal manipulation, ensure Oregon voters’ private data stays private, and stand up for state sovereignty and constitutional rights.
Trump’s DOJ thought they could bully states into compliance. They were wrong. With your support, we’ll keep proving them wrong. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
Thank you for standing with me in this fight,
Tobias Read
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250908em-data-fr]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)

Portland, OR 97242
United States

name: Coach Tuberville
subject: Your weekly sideline report from Coach Tuberville
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TUBERVILLE FOR GOVERNOR, INC
body:
Read now!

| [](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=9c98a09755d34e7c8c2fd5bc36ff23fd&_e=aqbFj6Mt716lWiuJeTvj5oa83JKLMjO-XCfSd1MHpb9bP6vd9TFIygPNuheHAxTKLMgD-KYvtDjniiDQEoEmaWQJNCVoi1mayt_TKGXLJMTmgjlhZJqOaOQPGV_eWKy-9qjI0xCIm0SQJIwsIBwT3rzkZHq9NDUcpYh8iwAMpSDzupr-iCYrI_Uq2AQRADti4mKrrI1jyX5kNcI_XMcfk5p7akjOIYcdayMjXNUgGl_9725cLJ8JHiddIFKeQ-_hjKeUO4u1CIwparQ_SSe8ckeRfCTLp7pAzYevpwbMJbGvJmdEETXJkaESYfq1RY3CKmpxft2-wf15oaG36JUFzAeXc21FdhPjZJEpzF5zrAxNDRv0cR65xnUoF1vEyCNp)
---
[ ](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=9c98a09755d34e7c8c2fd5bc36ff23fd&_e=aqbFj6Mt716lWiuJeTvj5oa83JKLMjO-XCfSd1MHpb9bP6vd9TFIygPNuheHAxTKLMgD-KYvtDjniiDQEoEmaYv-gXqthkdgtIGRY3K9grE0KZc_FYNqWWp3SM6wc6ef0PZD40-ysRATcoiPVaBvylUsdc_uDVpflCwGiAw6B022kqIJ90eCz2N-K320sureECcU3XI3PmfIBWkTki5gaDdid12L5_1XtRcO_eK5H82TR2WLnSkCwobbKeZqto0zNCudTHb26RSc65ejICq1m5C9CSrGaADk-eXty44QmXtSQRnCOvSMmc6k9DJM-lxF10cnle95QiwT-4Yw1yqT5nYyhkLZL6r23tz8qFQv4f6llYYCAzc_Mcm_RONleyZD)
Peter,

I know it's been a very heavy week for all of us. My hometown of Auburn was devastated by the senseless murder of Julie Gard Schnuelle, a beloved professor, last weekend. We continue to pray for her family and loved ones. We are also thankful for law enforcement, who responded quickly and apprehended the deranged suspect.

We are also heartbroken by the assassination of Charlie Kirk. Charlie was one of the strongest voices in the conservative movement and a true warrior who fought for his faith, his family, and his country. His commitment to finding solutions through open political dialogue is what made him so beloved by millions of Americans, especially by young people. There was no one more effective at connecting with the youth of America. Charlie and I became good friends over the last few years through his show, serving together on the Air Force Service Academy Board, and while working side by side to advance the conservative movement.

Charlie was a victim of political violence. His death should alarm every freedom-loving American in this country. Some members of the political class and the media have become too comfortable with labeling people they disagree with as "threats to America" or "threats to democracy", leading deranged people to resort to extreme measures. This hateful rhetoric threatens the livelihood of every American. Charlie was not part of the problem, he was part of the answer.

There is no doubt that Charlie's legacy of patriotism will live on and inspire the next generation. Suzanne and I join so many Alabamians in praying for Charlie's family and loved ones during this difficult time. In honor of Charlie, I will continue working every day to preserve the core values that make this country great - faith, family, and freedom.

**THE HUDDLE**
**THIS WEEK'S HEADLINES**

clean_body: Read now! | Peter, I know it's been a very heavy week for all of us. My hometown of Auburn was devastated by the senseless murder of Julie Gard Schnuelle, a beloved professor, last weekend. We continue to pray for her family and loved ones. We are also thankful for law enforcement, who responded quickly and apprehended the deranged suspect. We are also heartbroken by the assassination of Charlie Kirk. Charlie was one of the strongest voices in the conservative movement and a true warrior who fought for his faith, his family, and his country. His commitment to finding solutions through open political dialogue is what made him so beloved by millions of Americans, especially by young people. There was no one more effective at connecting with the youth of America. Charlie and I became good friends over the last few years through his show, serving together on the Air Force Service Academy Board, and while working side by side to advance the conservative movement. Charlie was a victim of political violence. His death should alarm every freedom-loving American in this country. Some members of the political class and the media have become too comfortable with labeling people they disagree with as "threats to America" or "threats to democracy", leading deranged people to resort to extreme measures. This hateful rhetoric threatens the livelihood of every American. Charlie was not part of the problem, he was part of the answer. There is no doubt that Charlie's legacy of patriotism will live on and inspire the next generation. Suzanne and I join so many Alabamians in praying for Charlie's family and loved ones during this difficult time. In honor of Charlie, I will continue working every day to preserve the core values that make this country great - faith, family, and freedom. THE HUDDLE THIS WEEK'S HEADLINES | (Jose Luis Magana/AP Photo) 9/09/2025 Sen. Tuberville demands faster action on stalled nominee confirmation. | (Manuel Balce Ceneta/AP Photo) 9/09/2025 Sen. name: Tim Kaine
subject: These VA Democrats need your support
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-hod [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-hod]
Derek,
This November, every seat in the Virginia House of Delegates is up for election, and our Democratic majority is razor-thin.
We must defend it — and we must expand it.
The stakes are high: If Republicans take back control of the House of Delegates, we can bet they’ll try to bring the same MAGA extremism we’ve seen in Washington here to Virginia. We can expect attacks on our core values like voting rights, an economy that works for all, reproductive freedom, and marriage equality — and we can’t let that happen.
That’s why I’m so proud to support these great Democrats running in key districts across Virginia. Every one of them is ready to fight for Virginians’ rights and values:
* Elizabeth Guzman (HD-22) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-guzman]
* Delegate Josh Thomas (HD-21) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-thomas]
* May Nivar (HD-57) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-nivar]
* Lily Franklin (HD-41) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-franklin]
* Delegate Joshua Cole (HD-65) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-cole]
* Leslie Mehta (HD-73) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-mehta]
* Jessica Anderson (HD-71) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-anderson]
* Lindsey Dougherty (HD-75) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-dougherty]
* Kimberly Pope Adams (HD-82) [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-adams]
But every one of them has a tough race ahead of them, especially with GOP billionaires from around the country pouring money into Virginia to fund Republican candidates. These great Dems need grassroots support to win, so I’m coming to you for help.
Please join me in supporting these strong Democrats on the ballot in November. You can split a donation between all of them by clicking here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-hod]
Or, if you’d like, you can click on any of their names above to support them directly.
Let’s make sure Virginia continues to lead the way forward — and send a powerful message that Trump’s extremism has no place here.
Thanks for always stepping up when it matters most.
Tim Kaine

clean_body: [] Derek, This November, every seat in the Virginia House of Delegates is up for election, and our Democratic majority is razor-thin. We must defend it — and we must expand it. The stakes are high: If Republicans take back control of the House of Delegates, we can bet they’ll try to bring the same MAGA extremism we’ve seen in Washington here to Virginia. We can expect attacks on our core values like voting rights, an economy that works for all, reproductive freedom, and marriage equality — and we can’t let that happen. That’s why I’m so proud to support these great Democrats running in key districts across Virginia. Every one of them is ready to fight for Virginians’ rights and values: Elizabeth Guzman (HD-22) [] Delegate Josh Thomas (HD-21) [] May Nivar (HD-57) [] Lily Franklin (HD-41) [] Delegate Joshua Cole (HD-65) [] Leslie Mehta (HD-73) [] Jessica Anderson (HD-71) [] Lindsey Dougherty (HD-75) [] * Kimberly Pope Adams (HD-82) [] But every one of them has a tough race ahead of them, especially with GOP billionaires from around the country pouring money into Virginia to fund Republican candidates. These great Dems need grassroots support to win, so I’m coming to you for help. Please join me in supporting these strong Democrats on the ballot in November. You can split a donation between all of them by clicking here. [] Or, if you’d like, you can click on any of their names above to support them directly. Let’s make sure Virginia continues to lead the way forward — and send a powerful message that Trump’s extremism has no place here. Thanks for always stepping up when it matters most. Tim Kaine [] CONTRIBUTE [] Paid for by Kaine for Virginia 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272 Williamsburg, VA 23185 Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115103993/569198855/823540626?refcode=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWQyMDlkOS0wMjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cycHxQ_NTUXzEOYSaukecaqXmeSHjqBL_wG3dexszSg=&emci=4e3b0c9a-e38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)
Hello Peter — From first grade through law school, I attended public schools right here in North Carolina. My mom was a public school teacher, and during my time in public schools, I was privileged to have teachers who cared about me, a family that supported me, and a broader community committed to providing an excellent public education for which I remain grateful. **My NC public school education put me on the path that has brought me here.** As a new school year gets underway, I want us all to bear in mind that our nation’s future leaders are learning in public schools today. Every student in every classroom across our country deserves the same opportunities that I had. **[I am making an urgent appeal today for you to stand with me in support of public education, Peter. It’s a bedrock of our society and a promise to future generations — but it is under attack. Will you add your name to oppose extreme GOP cuts to public education?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115103994/569198856/21623100?refcode=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWQyMDlkOS0wMjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cycHxQ_NTUXzEOYSaukecaqXmeSHjqBL_wG3dexszSg=&emci=4e3b0c9a-e38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115103995/569198857/21623100?refcode=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWQyMDlkOS0wMjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cycHxQ_NTUXzEOYSaukecaqXmeSHjqBL_wG3dexszSg=&emci=4e3b0c9a-e38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)

The federal government’s policies of slashing education funding, cutting school lunches, and otherwise undermining education are sowing chaos across the country, and they will harm students here in North Carolina and beyond.
I’m concerned for students who rely on Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) or other special education services. I worry for districts where far-right school voucher scams for the wealthy are siphoning resources away from the students most in need. And I am deeply troubled by the prospect of further cuts to come.
**[Join me and defend public schools, educators, and students. Add your name to my petition opposing far-right cuts to public education.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115103996/569198858/21623100?refcode=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWQyMDlkOS0wMjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cycHxQ_NTUXzEOYSaukecaqXmeSHjqBL_wG3dexszSg=&emci=4e3b0c9a-e38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=71d209d9-0290-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)**

Roy Cooper

name: Sharice for Congress HQ
subject: They want to make it impossible for a Democrat to win
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
We need to fight harder than ever before to make sure KS-03 voters aren’t silenced by Donald Trump’s latest gerrymandering scheme

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115104080/569199028/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMGQyZTEwMS0wMzkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HTE_n_VIOSumX1l2j-XCD6ZwR8vVsPpOFca2FN4g0M8=) For years, the Republican Party has turned to dirty tricks and shady super PACs to kick Sharice Davids out of office. But thanks to supporters like you, we’ve won each and every time. In 2018, National Republicans poured huge sums of money into her opponent’s campaign. **But we won — and we flipped KS-03 from red to blue.** And in 2021, the Kansas Legislature even tried to gerrymander Sharice’s seat, pushing thousands of her supporters into a neighboring district. **But in the next election, Sharice won by _even more._** Republican politicians have thrown _everything_ at the wall to take down Sharice. And every single time, this team has had her back. [_**Now, these politicians are trying to redistrict Kansas _again_ and turn KS-03 into a safe Republican seat. Can you chip in today to help us stop them?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115104081/569199029/-2097022923?refcode=email_091025_impossible&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMGQyZTEwMS0wMzkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HTE_n_VIOSumX1l2j-XCD6ZwR8vVsPpOFca2FN4g0M8=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115104082/569199030/-2097022923?refcode=email_091025_impossible&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMGQyZTEwMS0wMzkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HTE_n_VIOSumX1l2j-XCD6ZwR8vVsPpOFca2FN4g0M8=)
---
Listen, we want to be clear-eyed about what we’re up against: This new Republican gerrymander is beyond anything we’ve seen before. They don’t want to make KS-03 just _a little_ _more_ Republican like last time.
**They want to make it impossible for a Democrat to win. Not more difficult. Impossible.**
But this battle is just getting started. Over the coming weeks, we need to fight harder than ever before to make sure KS-03 voters aren’t silenced by Donald Trump’s latest gerrymandering scheme. And we can only do that with your help.

name: The Grant Institute
subject: Grant Report: September 12, 2025
body:
Election News Update from the Grant Institute

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/ca724591c564/grant-report-september-12-2025?e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| | | | | |

**Welcome to the Grant Institute’s Grant Report.** This periodic update highlights key developments impacting election administration, including voting litigation, policymaking, and federal activity.
The Ulysses S. Grant Institute (USGI) is a 501(c)(3) organization established alongside Restoring Integrity and Trust in Elections (RITE), dedicated to the study of voting and elections processes. USGI currently has several [scholarly projects](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=065cd3e464&e=c16f400a56) underway and, as our programming expands, we look forward to providing timely expert analysis and commentary on relevant topics through this update, as well as on [our website](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=b4b2a4d30c&e=c16f400a56) and social media channels.
We also welcome contributions. If you have news, commentary, or other relevant information to share, please submit to [info@grantdemocracy.org](mailto:info@grantdemocracy.org?subject=&body=) or to Janey Whitney, USGI’s Jaquish-Kenninger Fellow, at [janeywhitney@grantdemocracy.org](mailto:janeywhitney@grantdemocracy.org) for inclusion in the Grant Report. We are happy to promote studies, papers, projects, events, job openings, and other newsworthy items to those interested in voting and election administration.
Justin Riemer, President, USGI

---
|
---
| | **Alaska**
* Ten individuals born in American Samoa but now living in Whittier, Alaska, have [pleaded](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=47ce353f32&e=c16f400a56) not guilty to voter misconduct charges after allegedly registering or attempting to vote by falsely claiming U.S. citizenship. American Samoans are classified as U.S. nationals, not U.S. citizens, and therefore cannot vote in most U.S. elections. The case is related to Tupe Smith, who was arrested shortly after his 2023 election to a regional school board and now faces separate charges.

**Arizona**
* Arizona Republicans are [threatening](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=fc4a4fa29b&e=c16f400a56) to sue over the proposed Election Procedures Manual (EPM), which just concluded a 30-day public comment period, accusing Secretary of State Fontes of overstepping his authority, citing provisions that could allow apparent non-citizens extra time to prove citizenship along with other alleged problems. Others argue that some of the draft's guidance conflicts directly with Arizona law, and that only formal legislative changes could shift the rules. 2023’s EPM has been heavily litigated, with courts invalidating multiple components and one court ruling that the process of its adoption violated the state’s Administrative Procedures Act (see more below in Court Watch).

* Attorney General Kris Mayes issued an opinion declaring that voters affected by a state error of not collecting proof of citizenship, largely affecting those who received their driver’s license before October 1996 and registered to vote after 2004, cannot have their voter registrations downgraded or canceled for not responding to requests for documentation. State Attorney General opinions do not have the force of law.

**California**
* The California Supreme Court [rejected](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=31fde92a66&e=c16f400a56) a Republican emergency petition aimed at blocking Proposition 50, state Democrats’ plan to redraw congressional maps and potentially flip five GOP-held seats, from appearing on the November 4th ballot. This marks the court’s second dismissal of a legal challenge to halt the measure. There are additional pending legal challenges.

* [A 62-year-old California woman](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4777da2051&e=c16f400a56) has been charged with five felony counts after illegally registering her dog to vote and casting mail-in ballots in the dog’s name. Prosecutors say the dog’s ballot was accepted in the 2021 gubernatorial recall election but rejected in the 2022 primary. The case came to light when the woman self-reported in 2024, though investigators also pointed to social media posts where she showed her dog with an “I Voted” sticker and later noted the dog was still receiving ballots even after the dog had died. If convicted, she faces up to six years in prison. Read the [RITE Press Release ](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d056ef2e52&e=c16f400a56)[here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=fd28082b30&e=c16f400a56)[. ](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=61df81c4d2&e=c16f400a56)

**Florida**
* Florida's election in 2000, complete with hanging chads, butterfly ballots, and disputed recounts, is highly notable in election administration and election law. Over the following 25 years, the state overhauled its voting systems and streamlined procedures so effectively that it is now heralded as the "gold standard" in elections administration. The [James Madison Institute explains](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3813952df0&e=c16f400a56) how this impressive turnaround has come to define Florida's reputation in modern election integrity.

**Georgia**
* A Georgia House [Blue-Ribbon Study Committee on Election Procedures](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d47e73256f&e=c16f400a56) will review Georgia’s existing election laws, policies, and administrative practices. A [recent meeting](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e2ad1bee85&e=c16f400a56) examined the role of the State Election Board and heard member suggestions for improvements the legislature could enact. The committee will hold its next meeting in Savannah on September 18.

* Georgia legislators are weighing whether to roll back the [state’s automatic voter registration system](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5f72f90cfb&e=c16f400a56) after complaints of felon voter registration, duplicate applications, and inflated voter rolls. Georgia’s General Assembly session will begin in January of 2026.

**Hawaii**
* Kauaʻi [counted](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5605f834d4&e=c16f400a56) more drop‐box and mail‐in ballots than the county reports having collected in the 2024 general election. Exactly how many more? That’s where the real confusion lies: the state’s chief elections officer says the gap is round 25 ballots; the Hawaiʻi Supreme Court cited a discrepancy of 39 ballots; a state Elections Commission permitted interaction group found a discrepancy of 661; and Kauaʻi County’s initial envelope logs suggested a staggering gap of 3,772 ballots. The investigation draws on public records, court filings, and correspondence, which confirms inconsistencies across the board. The research alludes to possible weak links in the chain-of-custody records from collection through counting.

**Maine**
* A proposed Maine [ballot measure](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=82e8d68387&e=c16f400a56) would require voter photo ID and modify various absentee voting procedures.

[**Maryland**](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=40fe773bc0&e=c16f400a56)
* An Annapolis Alderman has [proposed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1dd39aac3f&e=c16f400a56) the city adopt ranked-choice voting. The legislation currently sits before the city council.

**Minnesota**
* The Minnesota Office of the Secretary of State is proposing [amendments to rules](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2f7ff07f01&e=c16f400a56) governing election administration. The proposed rules modify language related to voter registration; ballot formatting and instructions; absentee ballot administration; ballot safeguard procedures; mail balloting procedures; testing of voting systems; and election official training.

**Missouri**
* The Missouri Association of County Clerks and Election Authorities [announced](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ccac8853b3&e=c16f400a56) that the DOJ has reached out to at least two county clerks regarding the Dominion Voting Systems used in the 2020 election.

**Pennsylvania**
* In November, Pennsylvania will have [retention elections](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=26e1b2257a&e=c16f400a56) for three Supreme Court justices who must secure a “yes” vote to serve another 10-year term. This election is high-stakes as long-standing controversies over mail-in voting procedures, redistricting, and election logistics have recently made their way there. Historically, only one such justice has ever been ousted, but this year Republicans are campaigning aggressively for “no” votes, citing those rulings in election cases. If any justice fails to retain, the governor and state Senate would appoint a temporary replacement until an election is held in 2027.

* [Pennsylvania officials](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=bdf05be94b&e=c16f400a56) used data to uncover alleged double voting: a man who moved to Florida voted in person there and then also submitted a mail ballot in Bucks County in that same November 2020 election. The Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC), a multistate data-sharing consortium, is the method by which Pennsylvania collected the data and discovered the discrepancy. Prosecutors have filed charges following the discovery.

**Texas**
* The state legislature has enacted [H.B. 4 (PlanC2333)](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5836e30d6c&e=c16f400a56)[ ](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=769cf8a2ee&e=c16f400a56)which redraws congressional lines effective for the 2026 election. A federal court in El Paso has scheduled a [preliminary injunction hearing](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2a513f46db&e=c16f400a56) for Wednesday, October 1, in a case challenging the plan.

**Virginia**
* Albemarle County Police are [investigating](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2968430438&e=c16f400a56) Lauren Eddy, the former general registrar, over allegations of financial misconduct connected to her position. Eddy resigned on August 12, and the county Electoral Board has begun recruitment efforts to finish out Eddy’s unexpired term.

**West Virginia**
* Ohio County has [raised compensation](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5e6f8ac881&e=c16f400a56) for poll workers by $60, bringing total pay to $300 ($30 for training and $270 for Election Day), replacing the previous $20/$220 split. The raise is a proactive response to a nationwide poll worker shortage. The county needs nearly 300 workers to staff Election Day operations, which does not include early voting.

---
|
---
| | **Arizona** _**RNC v. Fontes,**_ No. CV2024-050553 (Maricopa Cnty. Superior Ct.). GOP organizations, supported by Restoring Integrity and Trust in Elections PAC, sued over Arizona’s 2023 Election Procedures Manual, alleging substantive and procedural violations of state law. After an initial dismissal, the Court of Appeals ruled on March 6, 2025 that Secretary of State Fontes violated the state APA. The Arizona Supreme Court granted review on August 20. On September 8, both [plaintiffs](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1bc55f4487&e=c16f400a56) and the [Secretary](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=304f47827f&e=c16f400a56) filed their supplemental briefs.
**Georgia** _**VoteAmerica v. Raffensperger**_ , No. 1:21-cv-01390 (N.D. Ga.). Progressive groups challenged Georgia’s S.B. 202, arguing that its restrictions on mailing prefilled or duplicative absentee ballot applications violated the First and Fourteenth Amendments. Republican organizations intervened to defend the law. After a 2024 trial, the court [upheld](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4078b0251c&e=c16f400a56) the challenged provisions on September 8, finding no constitutional violation.
**Idaho** _**March For Our Lives Idaho v. McGrane**_ , No. 1:23-cv-00107 (D. Idaho). Plaintiffs challenged Idaho’s prohibition of student ID to verify voter eligibility. The state won at the federal district court and plaintiffs appealed. Oral argument is scheduled in the Ninth Circuit on September 17, 2025.
**Illinois** _**Bost v. Illinois State Board of Elections**_ , No. 1:22-cv-02754 (N.D. Ill.). Rep. Mike Bost and two GOP elector nominees challenged Illinois's 14-day mail ballot receipt window. After being dismissed on standing grounds in the district court and the Seventh Circuit, they petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court, which granted review on June 2, 2025. Oral argument is set for October 8.
**New Hampshire** _**Robertson v. Scanlan**_ , No. 218-2025-CV-00951 (Rockingham Superior Ct.). On August 19, 2025, Plaintiffs filed [this lawsuit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e9ba009a34&e=c16f400a56) challenging SB 87 which requires voters to verify their identity in order to vote by mail, alleging voter burdens under the state constitution. A State Executive Council member is also under investigation for [allegedly](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a61e10b04e&e=c16f400a56) using her official email to refer would-be plaintiffs to Elias Law Group who is leading the litigation.
**North Carolina** _**North Carolina State Conference of the National Association of the NAACP v. Moore**_ , No. 18CVS009806-910 (Wake Cnty. Superior Court). On September 5, 2025, a trial court panel [upheld](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=179282ab51&e=c16f400a56) North Carolina’s Voter ID law. The litigation began in 2018 after the legislature passed legislation to implement a constitutional amendment to require voter ID that voters overwhelmingly approved. In 2022, the North Carolina Supreme Court ruled the law could be nullified on the grounds it was brought forward by legislators who were elected in racially gerrymandered districts.
_**United States v. North Carolina State Board of Elections**_ , No. 5:25-cv-00283 (E.D.N.C.). On September 9 a federal court [approved a legal settlement](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3fe5dd25f6&e=c16f400a56) between the Department of Justice and State Board of Elections, resolving a lawsuit over missing voter ID numbers in the state’s rolls. The agreement affirms the state’s ongoing [Registration Repair Project](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=efd8a66e21&e=c16f400a56), which aims to collect missing voter ID data (driver license number, or alternatively, last four digits of Social Security Number) in order to comply with the Help America Vote Act. Affected voters must vote provisionally, but their ballots count in federal races unless they are found ineligible for some other reason. For state and local contests, their ballots may not count if they fail to provide a matching ID number.
**Ohio** _**RedWine & Blue v. LaRose**_, No. 1:25-cv-01760 (N.D. Ohio). On August 22, 2025, Plaintiffs [filed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=43ef6ffd93&e=c16f400a56) this lawsuit challenging HB 54 which requires the state Bureau of Motor Vehicles to confirm individuals’ citizenship status before offering them a voter registration application, alleging violations under the National Voter Registration Act and federal constitution.
**Pennsylvania** _**Rossman v. Schmidt**_ , No. 516 MD 2024 (Pa. Comm. Ct.). A county commissioner, supported by RITE, seeks to enjoin Pennsylvania’s Department of State from enforcing a 2018 Directive instructing county officials to ignore inaccurate driver’s license or social security numbers on voter registration applications and to add these applicants to the voter registration rolls. Oral argument is scheduled October 7, 2025.
_**Eakin v. Adams County Board of Elections**_ , No.1:22-cv-340-SPB (W.D. Pa.). On August 26, the Third Circuit affirmed a lower court ruling striking down Pennsylvania’s mail ballot dating requirement. The RNC petitioned for a rehearing en banc on September 9.
_**Baxter v. Philadelphia Board of Elections**_ , No. 1305 C.D. 2024 (Pa. Comm. Ct.). Plaintiffs challenged Pennsylvania’s mail ballot dating requirements. On September 10, 2025, the Pennsylvania Supreme Court [held oral argument](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d16542304d&e=c16f400a56).
**Utah** The state legislature has made an [emergency request](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d21005ab6e&e=c16f400a56) to the state Supreme Court seeking to stay a state district court judge’s ruling that Utah’s congressional district boundaries were unconstitutional and ordering the state legislature to redistrict in time for the 2026 election.

---
| [Click here for more on RITE’s active litigation](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6cdc2f6965&e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| |
* After a call by President Trump to largely ban mail-in voting nationwide, he specifically [criticized](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6ae0bf17ce&e=c16f400a56) Colorado’s mail voting system while announcing the relocation of Space Command HQ from Colorado to Alabama.

* America First Legal Foundation [filed a petition](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=12b2f951ca&e=c16f400a56) with the Election Assistance Commission on July 16 requesting changes to EAC regulations and the National Mail Voter Registration Form that would mandate documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register for federal elections.

---
|
---
| |
* A n[ew study tested](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7451170e3e&e=c16f400a56) ways to encourage voters to verify their paper ballots produced by electronic Ballot Marking Devices (BMDs). Using mock elections with 60 undergraduates, researchers altered some printed ballots to see if voters would detect errors. A digital prompt reminding voters to carefully check their ballots and confirm accuracy boosted verification rates significantly, with 88% of participants catching anomalies. The study suggests that on-screen prompts can motivate voters to review both digital and printed ballots.

* The Bipartisan Policy Center has issued a report titled “[Modernizing List Maintenance](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=731405ff87&e=c16f400a56).”

* The Center for American Women and Politics at Rutgers-New Brunswick recently ran a [study](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f0c54eac8b&e=c16f400a56) that found beginning with the 1980 presidential election, women have consistently turned out to vote at higher rates than men, and their total number of votes has also exceeded that of male voters.

---
| |
---
|
---
| |
* **Director, Rutgers Democracy Lab**
[ View Position](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7758e8294a&e=c16f400a56)

---
|
---
| |
* Jason Snead, Executive Director for Honest Elections Project Action, provides commentary on recent election law changes regarding foreign influence in elections and a look at recent ranked-choice voting bans across six states. [Read here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=b1020d92d5&e=c16f400a56).

---
|
---
| |
* The Federal Election Commission has posted a list of preliminary [2026 Congressional Primary Dates](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=700935f9b2&e=c16f400a56).

* _Bost v. Illinois State Board of Elections_ : Supreme Court oral argument is scheduled for October 7, 2025.

* The [Democracy Project at NYU](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=361f210b89&e=c16f400a56) launched on September 10 with a series of “100 ideas in 100 days” from a range of varying perspectives.

---
|
---
