clean_body: Election News Update from the Grant Institute | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Welcome to the Grant Institute’s Grant Report. This periodic update highlights key developments impacting election administration, including voting litigation, policymaking, and federal activity. The Ulysses S. Grant Institute (USGI) is a 501(c)(3) organization established alongside Restoring Integrity and Trust in Elections (RITE), dedicated to the study of voting and elections processes. USGI currently has several underway and, as our programming expands, we look forward to providing timely expert analysis and commentary on relevant topics through this update, as well as on and social media channels. We also welcome contributions. If you have news, commentary, or other relevant information to share, please submit to mailto:info@grantdemocracy.org?subject=&body= or to Janey Whitney, USGI’s Jaquish-Kenninger Fellow, at mailto:janeywhitney@grantdemocracy.org for inclusion in the Grant Report. We are happy to promote studies, papers, projects, events, job openings, and other newsworthy items to those interested in voting and election administration. Justin Riemer, President, USGI | | | Alaska Ten individuals born in American Samoa but now living in Whittier, Alaska, have not guilty to voter misconduct charges after allegedly registering or attempting to vote by falsely claiming U.S. citizenship. American Samoans are classified as U.S. nationals, not U.S. citizens, and therefore cannot vote in most U.S. elections. The case is related to Tupe Smith, who was arrested shortly after his 2023 election to a regional school board and now faces separate charges. Arizona Arizona Republicans are to sue over the proposed Election Procedures Manual (EPM), which just concluded a 30-day public comment period, accusing Secretary of State Fontes of overstepping his authority, citing provisions that could allow apparent non-citizens extra time to prove citizenship along with other alleged problems. Others argue that some of the draft's guidance conflicts directly with Arizona law, and that only formal legislative changes could shift the rules. 2023’s EPM has been heavily litigated, with courts invalidating multiple components and one court ruling that the process of its adoption violated the state’s Administrative Procedures Act (see more below in Court Watch). Attorney General Kris Mayes issued an opinion declaring that voters affected by a state error of not collecting proof of citizenship, largely affecting those who received their driver’s license before October 1996 and registered to vote after 2004, cannot have their voter registrations downgraded or canceled for not responding to requests for documentation. State Attorney General opinions do not have the force of law. California The California Supreme Court a Republican emergency petition aimed at blocking Proposition 50, state Democrats’ plan to redraw congressional maps and potentially flip five GOP-held seats, from appearing on the November 4th ballot. This marks the court’s second dismissal of a legal challenge to halt the measure. There are additional pending legal challenges. has been charged with five felony counts after illegally registering her dog to vote and casting mail-in ballots in the dog’s name. Prosecutors say the dog’s ballot was accepted in the 2021 gubernatorial recall election but rejected in the 2022 primary. The case came to light when the woman self-reported in 2024, though investigators also pointed to social media posts where she showed her dog with an “I Voted” sticker and later noted the dog was still receiving ballots even after the dog had died. If convicted, she faces up to six years in prison. Read the Florida Florida's election in 2000, complete with hanging chads, butterfly ballots, and disputed recounts, is highly notable in election administration and election law. Over the following 25 years, the state overhauled its voting systems and streamlined procedures so effectively that it is now heralded as the "gold standard" in elections administration. The how this impressive turnaround has come to define Florida's reputation in modern election integrity. Georgia A Georgia House will review Georgia’s existing election laws, policies, and administrative practices. A examined the role of the State Election Board and heard member suggestions for improvements the legislature could enact. The committee will hold its next meeting in Savannah on September 18. Georgia legislators are weighing whether to roll back the after complaints of felon voter registration, duplicate applications, and inflated voter rolls. Georgia’s General Assembly session will begin in January of 2026. Hawaii Kauaʻi more drop‐box and mail‐in ballots than the county reports having collected in the 2024 general election. Exactly how many more? That’s where the real confusion lies: the state’s chief elections officer says the gap is round 25 ballots; the Hawaiʻi Supreme Court cited a discrepancy of 39 ballots; a state Elections Commission permitted interaction group found a discrepancy of 661; and Kauaʻi County’s initial envelope logs suggested a staggering gap of 3,772 ballots. The investigation draws on public records, court filings, and correspondence, which confirms inconsistencies across the board. The research alludes to possible weak links in the chain-of-custody records from collection through counting. Maine A proposed Maine would require voter photo ID and modify various absentee voting procedures. An Annapolis Alderman has the city adopt ranked-choice voting. The legislation currently sits before the city council. Minnesota The Minnesota Office of the Secretary of State is proposing governing election administration. The proposed rules modify language related to voter registration; ballot formatting and instructions; absentee ballot administration; ballot safeguard procedures; mail balloting procedures; testing of voting systems; and election official training. Missouri The Missouri Association of County Clerks and Election Authorities that the DOJ has reached out to at least two county clerks regarding the Dominion Voting Systems used in the 2020 election. Pennsylvania In November, Pennsylvania will have for three Supreme Court justices who must secure a “yes” vote to serve another 10-year term. This election is high-stakes as long-standing controversies over mail-in voting procedures, redistricting, and election logistics have recently made their way there. Historically, only one such justice has ever been ousted, but this year Republicans are campaigning aggressively for “no” votes, citing those rulings in election cases. If any justice fails to retain, the governor and state Senate would appoint a temporary replacement until an election is held in 2027. used data to uncover alleged double voting: a man who moved to Florida voted in person there and then also submitted a mail ballot in Bucks County in that same November 2020 election. The Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC), a multistate data-sharing consortium, is the method by which Pennsylvania collected the data and discovered the discrepancy. Prosecutors have filed charges following the discovery. Texas The state legislature has enacted redraws congressional lines effective for the 2026 election. A federal court in El Paso has scheduled a for Wednesday, October 1, in a case challenging the plan. Virginia Albemarle County Police are Lauren Eddy, the former general registrar, over allegations of financial misconduct connected to her position. Eddy resigned on August 12, and the county Electoral Board has begun recruitment efforts to finish out Eddy’s unexpired term. West Virginia Ohio County has for poll workers by $60, bringing total pay to $300 ($30 for training and $270 for Election Day), replacing the previous $20/$220 split. The raise is a proactive response to a nationwide poll worker shortage. The county needs nearly 300 workers to staff Election Day operations, which does not include early voting. | | | Arizona RNC v. Fontes, No. CV2024-050553 (Maricopa Cnty. Superior Ct.). GOP organizations, supported by Restoring Integrity and Trust in Elections PAC, sued over Arizona’s 2023 Election Procedures Manual, alleging substantive and procedural violations of state law. After an initial dismissal, the Court of Appeals ruled on March 6, 2025 that Secretary of State Fontes violated the state APA. The Arizona Supreme Court granted review on August 20. On September 8, both and the filed their supplemental briefs. Georgia VoteAmerica v. Raffensperger , No. 1:21-cv-01390 (N.D. Ga.). Progressive groups challenged Georgia’s S.B. 202, arguing that its restrictions on mailing prefilled or duplicative absentee ballot applications violated the First and Fourteenth Amendments. Republican organizations intervened to defend the law. After a 2024 trial, the court the challenged provisions on September 8, finding no constitutional violation. Idaho March For Our Lives Idaho v. McGrane , No. 1:23-cv-00107 (D. Idaho). Plaintiffs challenged Idaho’s prohibition of student ID to verify voter eligibility. The state won at the federal district court and plaintiffs appealed. Oral argument is scheduled in the Ninth Circuit on September 17, 2025. Illinois Bost v. Illinois State Board of Elections , No. 1:22-cv-02754 (N.D. Ill.). Rep. Mike Bost and two GOP elector nominees challenged Illinois's 14-day mail ballot receipt window. After being dismissed on standing grounds in the district court and the Seventh Circuit, they petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court, which granted review on June 2, 2025. Oral argument is set for October 8. New Hampshire Robertson v. Scanlan , No. 218-2025-CV-00951 (Rockingham Superior Ct.). On August 19, 2025, Plaintiffs filed challenging SB 87 which requires voters to verify their identity in order to vote by mail, alleging voter burdens under the state constitution. A State Executive Council member is also under investigation for using her official email to refer would-be plaintiffs to Elias Law Group who is leading the litigation. North Carolina North Carolina State Conference of the National Association of the NAACP v. Moore , No. 18CVS009806-910 (Wake Cnty. Superior Court). On September 5, 2025, a trial court panel North Carolina’s Voter ID law. The litigation began in 2018 after the legislature passed legislation to implement a constitutional amendment to require voter ID that voters overwhelmingly approved. In 2022, the North Carolina Supreme Court ruled the law could be nullified on the grounds it was brought forward by legislators who were elected in racially gerrymandered districts. United States v. North Carolina State Board of Elections , No. 5:25-cv-00283 (E.D.N.C.). On September 9 a federal court between the Department of Justice and State Board of Elections, resolving a lawsuit over missing voter ID numbers in the state’s rolls. The agreement affirms the state’s ongoing , which aims to collect missing voter ID data (driver license number, or alternatively, last four digits of Social Security Number) in order to comply with the Help America Vote Act. Affected voters must vote provisionally, but their ballots count in federal races unless they are found ineligible for some other reason. For state and local contests, their ballots may not count if they fail to provide a matching ID number. Ohio RedWine & Blue v. LaRose, No. 1:25-cv-01760 (N.D. Ohio). name: The New York Sun
subject: NY Times Fudges Coverage of Palestinian Terror Attack
body:
The New York Sun told you the truth.
| | | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cb41f1231ffd4c6dabff30c69ce12020&_e=YjRKFmzslKSTTAPRoKLiG8lqr13KwTgZcxarlM4ONcgHY9MFVIw79UPWwly0tMEnVQJPApVZGz9pp8bxnIeaPGSP9pxzYC3GfgifgZlYtQ-S_YcjLGfdTlCg-UyDsPrnJ_mCuTNgcfxAN-inQiPSdGlFxpSMLM0THI2t24yq9OQ3BxbYEMUvui1-I9DrkuvMPbZiq3T5KlqHreWwrBfjevboC_ZM_NM6MH-OpCY6lIDVB_hUU6dASGV0IyLUbk-rdt4vYBIiSt2pWHvdHB1N1lW04d2ZWV_Fpbx8cYkFoLU8-rglTJl1KSyNn9zWlbPboJLAOWyibftxmN9eKGxZcw%3D%3D)
---
|
---
| | FROM THE DESK OF THE PUBLISHER
---
| | Dear Reader,How bad is the New York Times? Well, how better to illustrate than to contrast the Times with The New York Sun?

**The screenshot on the left, from today, shows how the Times covered the story of a shocking terror attack in Israel. The Times makes no mention in the headline of who the perpetrators are. The Sun, as you can see below, wants you to know the truth. Nothing more. Nothing less.**
---
| | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cb41f1231ffd4c6dabff30c69ce12020&_e=YjRKFmzslKSTTAPRoKLiG8lqr13KwTgZcxarlM4ONcgHY9MFVIw79UPWwly0tMEnVQJPApVZGz9pp8bxnIeaPGSP9pxzYC3GfgifgZlYtQ-S_YcjLGfdTlCg-UyDsPrnJ_mCuTNgcfxAN-inQiPSdGlFxpSMLM0THI2t24yq9ORp5IdBSJc2PRP66mFyIiPVzCc8ZrZ9md49KoCVheZo7yZqEoDmYiaMaWMXThKUKEiq64iFElkW0e-kq_NWFJWfHWl73VB_KtwNZk9tLK3N1Hc6iX_wHRxFPrc387SDXYKlrIh4uq_PazjtxUmhKkldb7D0sKnsjIDXrNlFeCousw%3D%3D)
---
| | The difference couldn't be starker, but you already knew that. What you didn't know, perhaps, is what **you** can do about it. The answer is simple: [ Join The New York Sun today ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cb41f1231ffd4c6dabff30c69ce12020&_e=YjRKFmzslKSTTAPRoKLiG8lqr13KwTgZcxarlM4ONcgHY9MFVIw79UPWwly0tMEnVQJPApVZGz9pp8bxnIeaPGSP9pxzYC3GfgifgZlYtQ-S_YcjLGfdTlCg-UyDsPrnJ_mCuTNgcfxAN-inQiPSdGlFxpSMLM0THI2t24yq9ORCD2zzNOpQ1wxVeAD9AKP40ITzBIXWk5-tktGKdNTnL4AJ7jk5qe5R3jBYkvqNJfMHxLNF_XeXBwntOKVobcc_GP_gQmu6W6cGBDUclCIBrTlmNEE3b1iD4O-__xbmWlllrM7CTQ5bDMTL_9mHqZsqMbYi41x0XMD4dSaVw2y1nQ%3D%3D). Like you, we've screamed and shouted about the injustice of the Times claiming to be America's 'Paper of Record.' But now it's time to do something. It's time to join the movement to rebuild a better newspaper. It's time to join The New York Sun. Founded in 1833 - eighteen years before the Times - the Sun has been on the right side of history at every turn. The Sun is truly America's newspaper. It answers only to the American people. Well, how better to illustrate than to contrast the Times with The New York Sun? The screenshot on the left, from today, shows how the Times covered the story of a shocking terror attack in Israel. The Times makes no mention in the headline of who the perpetrators are. The Sun, as you can see below, wants you to know the truth. Nothing more. Nothing less. | | | | The difference couldn't be starker, but you already knew that. What you didn't know, perhaps, is what you can do about it. The answer is simple: . Like you, we've screamed and shouted about the injustice of the Times claiming to be America's 'Paper of Record.' But now it's time to do something. As political tensions continue to rise in the United States, Jay Jones pushes vicious rhetoric targeting Jason: **"You know him — you hate him."**

We say: You know Jason Miyares — **and he protects you anyway, whether you voted for him or not.** Because that’s the job of a public servant.

Jones wants to weaponize the Attorney General's office to punish political enemies and push a far-Left agenda. **He's already vowed to "take on" Virginians who disagree with him,** to go after police officers, and end mandatory sentencing for repeat drug dealers.

Even now — when our Commonwealth needs leaders who unite, not divide — Jones chooses political flames over healing.

**Jason Miyares? He doesn't punish people because they disagree. He protects them. All of them.**

This moment demands steady leadership, not dangerous rhetoric.

name: American Patriot Daily
subject: Ilhan Omar’s response to Charlie Kirk’s murder will leave you red with rage
body:
Turning Point USA founder Charlie Kirk’s assassination was one of the darkest days in the history of American politics.
---
[Mark as Spam](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xul=1nmc5a54x67zfmd5fi2bt1ih0gdygzkto8nrlcughqxhohbhrzjzcjic398jt61fuf1xb2674n7m4vgne36c7qmnpah7skvjf7t5oo3z5eke77vj3x9s&__stmp=t2hneh&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) | [Unsubscribe](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xul=1nmc5a54x67zfmd5fi2bt1ih0gdygzkto8nrlcughqxhohbhrzjzcjic398jt61fuf1xb2674n7m4vgne36c7qmnpah7skvjf7t5oo3z5eke77vj3x9s&__stmp=t2hneh&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)

name: Rev. Dr. Stephany Rose Spaulding
subject: The students are not alright
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POLITIVIST ACTION PAC
body:
https://www.movement4freedom.com/home [https://www.movement4freedom.com/home]
This week, Howard students staged a powerful walkout to protest Trump’s federal takeover of D.C.
They were joined by students from Georgetown, George Washington, and American University to demand that their schools resist cooperation with the illegal deployment of the National Guard and ICE.
And while Trump’s so-called “crime emergency occupation” technically expired at midnight on Wednesday, the danger is far from over. Congress is still serving up D.C. on a platter, keeping federal forces and over-policing in place, and denying our communities the right to real safety and freedom. That danger isn’t abstract, considering new threats to HBCU campuses fueled by MAGA hysteria rising in response to the assassination of Charlie Kirk, though absolutely no evidence points to such a justification.
These threats are designed to intimidate students and suppress their voices at the very moment they’re demanding to be heard.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act]
Howard students are showing immense courage in extremely dangerous times. We must support them and students everywhere...
The Movement for Freedom is on the ground, making sure these young leaders have the resources and support they need. But this fight is bigger than one walkout; it’s about building a future where safety and freedom belong to everyone. Our students deserve a demilitarized and safe college education, not one where going to class means being confronted by armed forces patrolling their neighborhoods.
Can you chip in today to power our work? Together, we can ensure that walking to class, to work, or down the block never puts anyone at risk. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act]
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act]
In solidarity,
Stephany
Lead Spokesperson, Movement for Freedom
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f_email-25q3?refcode=x_250912_HUstudents_act] Movement for Freedom is a campaign, founded by former Congresswoman Cori Bush, that calls for radically transforming how we build justice, safety, and liberation through love and solidarity. Every single dollar helps us build, train, and fight for the justice we all deserve.
Want to learn more? Visit our website at Movement4Freedom.com [https://www.movement4freedom.com] .

body:
This is Trump’s classic playbook, and we can’t let lawmakers get away with it.

Peter, Donald Trump knows he’s becoming a historically unpopular president — and that voters are eager to deliver him a damning referendum in the 2026 midterms. That is, if they get to weigh in fairly... So it’s no wonder, then, that he’s farming out his vice president to do his dirty work — sending J.D. Vance in to beg Indiana to rush a mid-decade gerrymander to help them cheat. In case you missed it, Vance flew in to meet with Indiana Governor Mike Braun to discuss replicating the strategy Texas Republicans just deployed to steal power from voters. As if to hammer the point home, they met behind a black curtain — putting on full display that this is a shady, backroom deal happening without the approval of voters. You can’t make this up, Peter: |
---
Then the White House summoned Indiana Republican lawmakers to D.C. to make sure they feel the pressure to do its bidding.
[**It couldn’t be clearer that Trump and his Republican allies are pulling out all the stops to try to rig the midterm elections. The NDRC is fighting back on all fronts, from Indiana to Texas to Missouri to wherever Republicans try to gerrymander next. Hey folks,
Joe Walsh and I went deep on the show this week to process the shock, grief, and fallout from Charlie Kirk's assassination. Whether you agreed with him or not, what happened was horrifying. We got honest about our own complicated relationships with Kirk, the dangerous rise in political violence, and what this moment demands from all of us.
* **01:13** -- Joe reflects on his personal history with Charlie Kirk, calling him "like a son," and unpacks the painful split caused by Trumpism.
* **03:00** -- I talk about mourning someone you fundamentally disagree with--and why showing compassion doesn't mean absolving hate.
* **09:00** -- We break down what we know about the shooter, including the bizarre bullet inscriptions and early signs of radicalization.
* **14:01** -- We explore the root causes of this violence: disaffected men, online extremism, and the loneliness epidemic no one wants to solve.
* **26:12** -- Joe sounds the alarm on the influencer takeover of government--and what it means when provocateurs are setting national policy.

Don't miss this one. This is an emotionally raw, politically urgent conversation about where we go from here. If you care about saving our democracy from the forces trying to rip it apart, this is essential viewing.

With urgency,
--Mike

Whether you agreed with him or not, what happened was horrifying. We got honest about our own complicated relationships with Kirk, the dangerous rise in political violence, and what this moment demands from all of us. 01:13 -- Joe reflects on his personal history with Charlie Kirk, calling him "like a son," and unpacks the painful split caused by Trumpism. 03:00 -- I talk about mourning someone you fundamentally disagree with--and why showing compassion doesn't mean absolving hate. 09:00 -- We break down what we know about the shooter, including the bizarre bullet inscriptions and early signs of radicalization. 14:01 -- We explore the root causes of this violence: disaffected men, online extremism, and the loneliness epidemic no one wants to solve. 26:12 -- Joe sounds the alarm on the influencer takeover of government--and what it means when provocateurs are setting national policy. Don't miss this one. This is an emotionally raw, politically urgent conversation about where we go from here. If you care about saving our democracy from the forces trying to rip it apart, this is essential viewing. ** With urgency, --Mike | A guest post by| Walsh is a former U.S. Congressman and conservative talk radio host who waged a Republican primary challenge against Donald Trump in 2020. He is currently the director of The Social Contract and host of the "White Flag with Joe Walsh" podcast. Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. You 're currently a free subscriber to Endless Urgency with Mike Nellis. For the full experience or to support my work so I can keep it free for as many people as possible, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Mike Nellis 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: All eyes on New Jersey
body:
Friend,

Every single day since he took office in January, it seems like Donald Trump has found new ways to abuse his power and make life harder for our families.

* He’s illegally withheld funding that New Jerseyans rely on for child care, for health care, and for access to healthy food.
* He’s deployed the National Guard to American cities in a transparent attempt to instill terror and fear.
* He signed a devastating spending bill that guts Medicaid access and makes everything from groceries to heating your home less affordable — all to give billionaires a giant tax cut.

And honestly, that’s just a fraction of it.

As Trump has inflicted all this damage on our country, Republican politicians like Jack Ciattarelli have cheered him on every step of the way.

There is seemingly nothing Trump can do — no matter how illegal, reckless, or outright devastating for New Jersey families — that would make Jack Ciattarelli say “enough is enough.”

And friend, polls show that Ciattarelli is within a few percentage points of becoming New Jersey’s next governor.

If we’re going to win this race and prevent that from happening, we need to work night and day these next 10 weeks to get our message out.

I intend to do exactly that, friend — because the stakes for New Jersey, and for our nation, are too high to give it anything less. But I can’t win this race without your help.

Let’s send a message that the people reject Donald Trump and Jack Ciattarelli’s disastrous agenda, friend. Let’s send a message that we won’t stand for their attacks on working families or on the rule of law — and that we’re ready to lead the way with a better path forward. Please rush a split donation between my campaign and New Jersey’s Nellie Pou today before our September end-of-month deadline. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/np_ms_em_splt&refcode=250912_ms_sherill]

Donate [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/np_ms_em_splt&refcode=250912_ms_sherill]Thank you,
Mikie
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pou_headshot_crop.jpg]

About Nellie Pou
Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=1de5da4e-0890-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=1de5da4e-0890-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413]
Follow Nellie

Hey there,
The Wisconsin Supreme Court race has entered a new phase.
Our former opponent, Justice Rebecca Bradley, announced she’s not running for reelection.
Now, right-wing special interests are scheming to recruit and bankroll another extreme candidate to try to tilt the court in their favor.

We need to prepare now.

name: Team Caraveo
subject: Dr. Yadira Caraveo Withdraws Candidacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/92508/images/Caraveo%20Logo.jpg]
THORNTON, CO - Today, Democrat Dr. Yadira Caraveo announced that she will be withdrawing her candidacy from the US House race in Colorado’s 8th Congressional District. She released the following statement:
"This was not a decision I made lightly. It was a privilege representing the 8th district in Congress and working for the people of the district, Colorado, and the country. Unfortunately, I faced very strong resistance to my candidacy this cycle due almost entirely to the stigmatization of mental health in America. I hope that one day we will see more acceptance and understanding of illnesses like depression, and that leaders at all levels will be able to support those in need in actions and not just words. I want to personally thank everyone who voted for me, donated to my campaign, believed in me, volunteered for me, worked for me, and supported me. I urge all my supporters to continue fighting for change."
– Dr. Yadira Caraveo
Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add info@caraveoforcongress.com to your address book.
This campaign is powered by grassroots donations. You can click here [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail] to make a donation to support Yadira Caraveo for CO-08. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you no longer want to receive email updates, unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tGPOegMQMEaYhe93A2byqA2?unsubscribedata=xrQL9JxxEi8XjrToBt814omQYpvGWUAYpnvQKIb%2B6uk2nbOKCraTd16uwgTMjRSI5%2FotuQmeY6Y1h1EA5Vw9CPuji00G%2Fg3xfZmrWlGGijGdIeqcy6VYctITJbS0yv2euiQMvizVi14%2B%2BZWze%2FtJyKcFS%2F1mh863zid5nP0onGwCrUErYAU0A9ETmM1Sbd0ziT3vnEdlQmLXQol7cqg6BONq8XqnXpJcxa6CWWtoIWrgZAR6lgB%2FAyBk9SLIS3gwBTxRslHhTc5DkWxeUYD2osxBglfPybra1VPLBfNUH6pZGp9tSOQBSMsUbaW2Z3FiFd0SoCTUSy6oGG1I83Sl%2BJD3jcXzw02q9wCi6PTvBmqFLe%2BS3woVUz%2Bn2d0B3Y%2F5z%2BRJiP%2BCMjPJVpcXuLc15PbMqL297meJbMvxrfDaT2SX5HDBrgFwV2f%2B%2BiHID7eb .

| Good morning Friend,
This is the **final call** for applications to the **Mississippi Leadership Academy (MSLA).** We already have an incredible group of students signed up, and we’re looking for just a few more to round out our class. If you’ve been considering this for your child or grandchild, now is the time! **Why MSLA is worth it:**
* **Completely free** to apply and participate
* Just **one Saturday a month for two months**
* **Volunteer hours signed off** (perfect for college or scholarship applications)
* **Proven impact:** 35% of past students have landed internships with leaders they met through MSLA — including the State Auditor and Attorney General
* Access to **statewide elected officials, legislators, and community leaders**

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Pray for our Country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
| | | |
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend, It’s been a heavy week. There’s no sugarcoating it.

Charlie Kirk loved our God, his family, and his country. Charlie Kirk was a true American patriot that influenced a generation in a way that few in our history have been able to. Charlie Kirk was assassinated on Wednesday while standing up for his beliefs.
---
[](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/WJBA6qMlJPS8R09vl5OfSSKrPzJ0YWL2UzXEjSUahPG9uohA3j7TU20XnJ9LnMZrFebk5CLCX_qGH9b3YQzsuCo34EXUVDwkVBnoK4T79XtFSOQTLic7zyw2bHg7Ho1rjEm3N9YhA78vUQfckJtd9qqrYwgyBOXiLDvScZkrJThoE-GKy_Ti4M2YLaIvFSsx-Q_mpjzNibiwQODjXnQ5P_QHhaEuU1x06QKs3PQJg_MgCtLuJYZm8D92gOAtWh5R6B8jf7WZ6r0vHBiqLbOjZGtRt-KxGqf8iq9THGv7FU8vt2F-SxyN3Kfchll_6U_KjqL3DtLIas2S22sjQ9zsy7xFl82oB2uucKC2_PCmH80shA0wkJc_87CUvk8GDhGcqD5xO85m-dZYpzqKwMAWWO1cPxzM/2ugjrzvYzIJbth40u0cmWSeSCbIUwet8/12)
---
While rational humans mourned in the moments following this heinous act of terrorism, the Left celebrated. MSNBC suggested that it may have just been a "[supporter firing a celebratory round](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/0g1RaiKK40ZWrBHWIj0XvCu2sdiiReonieraiJuae4V14i8F99UGUZB7haIQ4NbHqw4YP2ouKFPWKgu-N4YVfii1SaCmm73AoM6yFJE4-1Mz-t2f7Eopq80FCux244AX5T2iDd9Ufc1DBJg8B8JHFcvkgdkcYG4wNGJLNkNpwyaK9PFOIh41TxGHa-3DJfbTnia9VTFgJ50pCf_NOmL1zb5ZJVGh5-LEpLyKlO5Tg4NHUkZz4WZIyHndVNrF2NqKJNJ0MJg6B5R8U0XoQWor73sB6_6RlEs1NYbzxhNcXObyEt_moXPTl4meYkuHsqD0RV27-pNdyvxYO4QCwX5ijsVAHyA_skFqaSsqbXCYRcML5yMkmKFX9Bu-xB-GdaPPPlydenff2Fhj396-zAWKpt4Fq3Oo/ntbcyDbvAtV658vy9JTDfAkaKwva38ZN/12).” My colleagues on the left booed and shouted “NO” [as prayer was requested on the House floor](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/pSj_BCHO5j_Yl1yg1PuDi2pSkSi2GJfW8Nwno11OOPaSaz3KJckkvDNvu1CtGxNNDPr4O63FMfUK9e3e-hX090DspFS38JSTAFDhqWO8dMEgcAwCnLBiRkTQQd8ZZ9E2zNelwwwT_4m5RH9b7lwKWfHjGNq78viEZn84YCiYA-HWU6T7sicxX_MNjvJgTb08ck8LvXTmRlOiY95QxRonKtzFHZr5_9b1gb3iLcNhxWv0vt5IEiuTxfYsrARIrhFN9TVFiiO7Yzs49iY6Mw7kG8x1xv-a_UWzHcr2G8zeBa4wCSPYhD14Ri0vR9uWCJwcYyQ3JFY7Gm8O5Kp5ARfkG5xpveY_JIzb0AlZUCTEnJBzwBculpaYGqAnkzJ4MhxLeQHMbW2-P0zK1ePyXNEVmjsMZHp5ILRmBBQC4YD7P0JApmdriVlzHIXhHAwRzMk/sqmFgTDEwS5TRY1nvTCiMGrRugKzDwzQ/12). A Facebooker commented “[Your god didn’t help. Sorry](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/ILDAaIZPhyJoLHm1sNJB6_eOjnHyHoq7YnKuunTeFU609GZfJdy4COGJC1-kGD0M35AnsAdTLhlltGfq8_en-6RN2EfJMS6iU0DDZwvCpXIkyaUVmERAD84weUOKU5t616V0vGW-b3seQWxoz_sV187xlrRbb0b1qZhC-QKSNXlKqwLewlT5bY9b2_HPSsTU5btC367WScD1Ns9i00YzuhP5M4mxem7KozhyoxZAWZlk5EOiRRKEplvwTLI84IC1jvP5HE1z-Z07xXOmc3YeMiCNOSRRxWuophK-gQpfTeCUbHUbybYipWG57KoLBgrFVXKiTvAw2AjHRnPQIz8yMVKVxyGW7WN-AKV9tNvT4ltOsJ-z09Jd1-nWyQhl-ZQ-XBL3pRIBSlFm6WP4ch5Kj3NvpAEuy2k4XdNe6ww3kM-UMRQO34lRniJb1AEsl06I6VbX12A/5_Zf8zldXd3jtliQHGlmggxXPgOMg8bj/12).”
---
[](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/i-xBaoaRYSQ7zLXyyVtM6rAhTv90qTNPYZOqUUwxQl0m3Gdfeo_6rLt-8nHe7zZtsDLCj_slTzUt1JD5Db1gagD92lUNEykiL0lctHRsN7wqlEh7t5D9rJT_78bfiAQkvYHIVeUJMuY3xguvm7pI9gT51gfKPwlj4ynBF--GL4ZY9U7FFKFii7StznG_A3OhgH4ZbHC87_TFiSQMhrOEt85JsaEOOCwLVccT_j_gjlFihoKBZCQyGIMLxniJVQGH4HKfRYD66HKkggVTLGRvAfa9wNXR0PmoXQu-8hcStsW1k0_ADD4rL-ii6qzIaSPHDjUXqyuFwy3ocVcoA4zSKv3syY0dO5QkmfZ-HkGo2qAsiLZoMWIpluTz6FogVwoef43Es0vBvtS2wxbIoSuN-8kZDiYc/mTN673yuEozy47x_lNVQBNuaIGFzMlxe/12)
---
What has happened to our Country? On the night of the 1992 presidential election, my daddy, the World War II Marine and toughest guy I knew, prayed a very simple prayer: “Lord, please don’t let us lose our country.” Pray for Erika Kirk and Charlie’s two young children who are going to grow up with stories, not memories. Pray for the families of the thousands of Americans who lost their lives on [September 11, 2001](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/n6IJ-ZggOydwmXZSbwbFnETTeb_VFkyA_3HZJQYoKfgZh8MpcPmy1yOe256pOU9RBrfGH_hoNGxjWVuX61iXh72ACk0lmvb3j7eHtUVjdAzvxHfQ1RE9FOiPi3vCMQePjx5QbO5GpE3574eeFF3oDxIMU0HWgw5QDZKTBcwRwRCiPoL_fIF0pA-0VVO-WYdTIPgeFrcPgLJ8PZwECvk8C5Vk4HfNJFHcwnOUH_GmJVfSTdCvG8PdHegwSz30NraxE8yID8ttIS6k0We31rO6P-0DTaMu0MQfScCKt6JQPI6p7F-xDEgEp6dHPDVBF8x_vm0FcJ34XOaVby5c0CDjyMpWNfvoaC2OMydxMEH3aSfA0gGEYK3GyFsqp27X0ivfystxlydth-M0V3GMFIxqiGDxzcOBfusbWZd8GBShn69DTY2zTBPFD02ZseCm1bI/OzU5Nitod7NNoNZAMgCfLHe9YnXlt0Qy/12). Pray for the family of Iryna Zarutska, the innocent 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee whose life was taken by a man who should have never been on the streets. “Lord, please don’t let us lose our country.” -Tim
---
| | |
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | Dear Friend, It’s been a heavy week. There’s no sugarcoating it. Charlie Kirk loved our God, his family, and his country. Charlie Kirk was a true American patriot that influenced a generation in a way that few in our history have been able to. Charlie Kirk was assassinated on Wednesday while standing up for his beliefs. While rational humans mourned in the moments following this heinous act of terrorism, the Left celebrated. MSNBC suggested that it may have just been a ".” My colleagues on the left booed and shouted “NO” . A Facebooker commented “.” What has happened to our Country? On the night of the 1992 presidential election, my daddy, the World War II Marine and toughest guy I knew, prayed a very simple prayer: “Lord, please don’t let us lose our country.” Pray for Erika Kirk and Charlie’s two young children who are going to grow up with stories, not memories. Pray for the families of the thousands of Americans who lost their lives on . Pray for the family of Iryna Zarutska, the innocent 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee whose life was taken by a man who should have never been on the streets. “Lord, please don’t let us lose our country.” -Tim | | | Stay in Touch 👇 Paid for by Burchett for Congress | | | ---|---|---

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 9/12/2025 💔
body:
Remembering Charlie Kirk, plus updates on Evergreen
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Dear Readers,
This has been one of the hardest weeks we’ve ever faced at Rocky Mountain Voice. Charlie Kirk wasn’t just a national leader—he was a personal friend to both Heidi and Tori, [a friendship that began 10 years ago](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) with their own fights at both University of Oregon and CU Boulder. His [horrific murder](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) has left us devastated, grieving alongside so many of you who felt connected to the movement he built.
What’s become clear in these dark days is the stark divide in America. Some have [disgracefully celebrated his death](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), exposing the depth of hatred against truth and faith in this country. But at the same time, we’ve seen something stronger: **a rising wave of conviction.** Millions are standing taller in their faith, more determined than ever to continue the mission Charlie started.
[As J.D. Vance said](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), Charlie wasn’t just a thinker—he was a doer. He lit a fire in a generation, and that fire isn’t going out.
**[On Sunday, Colorado will gather.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** At 2 p.m. at BRAVE Church in Englewood and Westminster, friends, family, and leaders from across the state—including Pastor Jeff, Jeff Hunt, Victor Marx, and Heidi—will speak at a memorial service to honor his life. If you can, please join us. [You can RSVP here.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)

**Latest Updates on the assassination of Charlie Kirk:**
* The suspect, [22-year-old Tyler Robinson](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), has been arrested and will face charges soon. Read more about the rifle and writings the FBI found [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
* On Friday, Air Force 2 delivered Charlie’s body home to Arizona, where he will be laid to rest.
* President Trump has posthumously awarded him the Medal of Freedom, the nation’s highest civilian honor, calling him a “warrior for truth.”

But tragedy didn’t stop there. It also struck here in Colorado, where a [shooting at Evergreen High School](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) left two students critically wounded and a community in shock. Families are clinging to hope, and we’re reminded again how fragile life is.
At the same time, the nation paused to remember September 11. [Thousands gathered at Red Rocks](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to climb in honor of the nearly 3,000 Americans killed that day—a moment that united a generation in patriotism and faith.
**Now, 24 years later, this week feels like another turning point.** For our generation, and especially for the young people Charlie Kirk inspired, it’s time to choose courage over fear, conviction over compromise, and truth over silence.
We can’t undo the tragedies of this week. But we can decide how to respond. Charlie once said the greatest threat to tyranny is a generation _unafraid of the truth_. His voice has been silenced, but his mission lives on in us.
Let’s rise together. We will keep fighting—for truth, for freedom, and for Colorado. It's what Charlie would have wanted.
**📌 _More coverage from RMV:_** [A Nation Mourns: The Unthinkable Murder of Charlie Kirk and the Politics of Silence](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) [Charlie Kirk’s Legacy of Faith, Courage and Conviction](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) [The Courage of a Calling](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) [Colorado Political and Civic Leaders Denounce Assassination of Charlie Kirk](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) [Remembering Charlie Kirk: Colorado memorial service set for Sunday](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, September 12, 2025.
– Team RMV
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[From Oregon to Boulder – The early fights that shaped Charlie Kirk](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Some leaders visit a place. Charlie Kirk kept coming back. Heidi Ganahl saw it up close.
“Charlie always saw hope in Colorado... he never gave up on Colorado. He always came here,” she said. “He was one of the only ones that really stood strong with me when I was getting so vilified in the governor’s race... a great mentor and teacher to me.”
>From her vantage point as a CU Regent and a candidate, his loyalty was unmistakable. But his roots with Colorado’s conservative movement stretch back even further, to one of the first college students he ever backed: Heidi’s daughter Tori.
The first spark came in 2016 after a chance introduction. Tori recalls that her grandpa sat next to Charlie at an Leadership of the Rockies retreat. The next day her phone rang. “Hi, my name’s Charlie. I just sat next to your grandpa. I heard you’re Panhellenic president. Heard you’re conservative.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Evergreen Community Mourns After Deadly High School Shooting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [KDVR FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A shooting at Evergreen High School left at least two students critically wounded and the suspect deceased by suicide.
* Freshman Sam Fisher described [hiding in the band room](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), seeing the shooter point a gun through the door, and said classmates reported the suspect smiling and waving during the attack.
* The Evergreen community is grieving the tragedy, with parents demanding accountability and students expressing fear about returning to school, noting this marks yet another school shooting in Colorado since Columbine.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Henry Ford’s vaccine study backfired, and parents weren’t supposed to see it](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**BY JEN SCHUMANN | NATIONAL | SEPTEMBER 10** _Parents have long been told that the science on vaccines is settled. A study conducted inside Henry Ford Health in Detroit set out to reinforce that message. Its authors wrote that their goal was to “reassure parents of the overall safety of vaccination.”_
__
**[Mourning or Margin? What the Silence Over Iryna Zarutska’s Murder Reveals About Media, Policy and Political Deadlock](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY C. J. GARBO | COMMENTARY | SEPTEMBER 10** _The brutal and senseless murder of 23‐year‐old Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte light rail train has been met not with widespread grief, but with unsettling silence. When a story like this fades from national coverage, especially after being shared widely on social platforms, it raises urgent ethical and political questions – questions the mainstream media seems unwilling to confront._
__
**[Day of Defense on Sept. 20 equips families to fight exploitation](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN | LOCAL | SEPTEMBER 9** _Colorado ranks tenth in the nation for human trafficking, despite being only 21st in population. That reality is what Abby Corrigan, CEO of the Colorado Faith Alliance, says drives her organization’s work. She believes the fight begins with a clear truth._
__

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat
|
---
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** “If you believe in something, you need to have the courage to fight for those ideas - not run away from them or try and silence them.” - Charlie Kirk

name: Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
subject: In Memory of September 11th and Charlie Kirk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
body:
We Will Not Quit. We Will Not Break.

| | | | | [|
---](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=1e10c3733d&e=4f81ceec6f)
| | | | | | **September 11th, 2001****In Honor** On this day, we remember the fallen of September 11th, 2001. Nearly 3,000 lives were taken in a single morning, and America was forever changed. We honor the men and women who never came home, and we give reverence to the heroes who ran toward danger: our firefighters, police officers, EMTs, medical workers, military personnel, and every first responder who placed duty above self.
The Republican Party in the Virgin Islands joins the nation in prayer for the families who still bear the weight of loss. Their courage remains our inspiration. Their sacrifice remains our charge: to defend freedom, cherish unity, and never forget.
_We remember. We honor. We will never forget._
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=c59b8a774e&e=4f81ceec6f)
| | **Charlie Kirk** We are heartbroken by the loss of Charlie Kirk. A Christian, a husband, a father of two, and a warrior for truth. The world is quieter without his voice. Our movement mourns.
Charlie began Turning Point USA at 18 years old. From those early days, he dedicated himself to awakening America’s youth and rallying a nation back to faith and freedom. He leaves behind legions of sorrowful supporters across America and beyond. Martyred for the truth he spoke so boldly, his fight is ours. His example lights our path. His courage strengthens our steps.
We will carry his torch forward, with the Grace of Our Lord before us. Charlie Kirk will be remembered, and his impact will endure for generations.
_Rest in Glory, Charlie. We will not quit. We will not stop. We will keep pushing._
---
| |
**From the Heart of the Chairman** Yesterday, we paused in solemn remembrance. September 11th reminds us of the price of freedom and the courage of those who stood in harm’s way. We honor every life lost and every first responder whose bravery carried our nation through the darkness of that day. This week, we also grieve the passing of Charlie Kirk. Charlie was more than a leader. He was a husband, a father, a man of faith, and a warrior for truth. His influence reached across America and into the U.S. Virgin Islands, strengthening our resolve to defend liberty with boldness and conviction.Though our hearts are heavy, we will not falter. Charlie’s impact lives on in us, and we will carry his torch forward, working harder, standing stronger, and speaking louder for the values he held dear.Charlie will be missed. We remain faithful to the cause you championed. We are reminded of the fragility of life and the strength required to defend freedom. Let us move forward together with resolve, guided by our values and our love for country.
_Chairman John P. Yob_
---
| | **From the National Committeewoman** On this day of remembrance, we honor those lost on September 11th, and we also honor Charlie Kirk, a voice for faith and liberty. May their legacies strengthen us as we continue the work before us. As a wife and mother, my prayers are for his wife and his children. My prayers are with them all.
_Mrs. April Newland_
---
| | **From the National Committeeman** September 11th reminds us of America’s resilience. The loss of Charlie Kirk reminds us of the power of one life lived with conviction. Both call us to action: to defend freedom, to cherish faith, and to serve our country without hesitation.
_Jim Hughes_
---
| | **From the Executive Director** For me, this week has been one of grief and reflection. September 11th will forever mark our nation’s strength through suffering. Charlie Kirk’s passing has left me personally shaken. He gave voice to truths many feared to speak, and his courage strengthened my own. Through all this, we do not stop. We press on. We speak out and stand together. There is no retreat._As State Committee Member, Barbara de la Portilla, reminds us: ‘We don’t get to choose what time we leave this earth. But you do get to decide your impact while you’re here.’_ May truth guide us all forward. Courageously.
_Andrea Hedrington_
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
# **By the President of the United States of America **
September 10, 2025 _Honoring the Memory of Charlie Kirk_
**A Proclamation** As a mark of respect for the memory of Charlie Kirk, by the authority vested in me as President of the United States by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, I hereby order that the flag of the United States shall be flown at half-staff at the White House and upon all public buildings and grounds, at all military posts and naval stations, and on all naval vessels of the Federal Government in the District of Columbia and throughout the United States and its Territories and possessions until sunset, September 14, 2025. I also direct that the flag shall be flown at half-staff for the same length of time at all United States embassies, legations, consular offices, and other facilities abroad, including all military facilities and naval vessels and stations._In Witness Whereof_ , I have hereunto set my hand this tenth day of September, in the year of our Lord two thousand twenty-five, and of the Independence of the United States of America the two hundred and fiftieth.
DONALD J. TRUMP

clean_body: We Will Not Quit. We Will Not Break. | | | | | | | | | | | September 11th, 2001In Honor On this day, we remember the fallen of September 11th, 2001. Nearly 3,000 lives were taken in a single morning, and America was forever changed. We honor the men and women who never came home, and we give reverence to the heroes who ran toward danger: our firefighters, police officers, EMTs, medical workers, military personnel, and every first responder who placed duty above self. The Republican Party in the Virgin Islands joins the nation in prayer for the families who still bear the weight of loss. Their courage remains our inspiration. Their sacrifice remains our charge: to defend freedom, cherish unity, and never forget. We remember. We honor. We will never forget. | | | | | | | | | | | | Charlie Kirk We are heartbroken by the loss of Charlie Kirk. A Christian, a husband, a father of two, and a warrior for truth. The world is quieter without his voice. Our movement mourns. Charlie began Turning Point USA at 18 years old. From those early days, he dedicated himself to awakening America’s youth and rallying a nation back to faith and freedom. He leaves behind legions of sorrowful supporters across America and beyond. Martyred for the truth he spoke so boldly, his fight is ours. His example lights our path. His courage strengthens our steps. We will carry his torch forward, with the Grace of Our Lord before us. Charlie Kirk will be remembered, and his impact will endure for generations. Rest in Glory, Charlie. We will not quit. We will not stop. We will keep pushing. | | From the Heart of the Chairman Yesterday, we paused in solemn remembrance. September 11th reminds us of the price of freedom and the courage of those who stood in harm’s way. We honor every life lost and every first responder whose bravery carried our nation through the darkness of that day. This week, we also grieve the passing of Charlie Kirk. Charlie was more than a leader. He was a husband, a father, a man of faith, and a warrior for truth. His influence reached across America and into the U.S. Virgin Islands, strengthening our resolve to defend liberty with boldness and conviction.Though our hearts are heavy, we will not falter. Charlie’s impact lives on in us, and we will carry his torch forward, working harder, standing stronger, and speaking louder for the values he held dear.Charlie will be missed. We remain faithful to the cause you championed. We are reminded of the fragility of life and the strength required to defend freedom. Let us move forward together with resolve, guided by our values and our love for country. Chairman John P. name: Michael Bennet
subject: My story and why I'm running
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
Let me explain.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106852/569209053/-2054079050?refcode=jtk893-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTRkOWE2MC0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZzamQz8pvkRvwr6TbJLuiEb1WdY_bJ10H7xzAtY6o7E=)
---
Friend, when my mom and grandparents came to America, it was a beacon of hope and opportunity – and a chance to rebuild their lives.
**To them, America was a place where, if you worked hard, you could build a life, live in peace and freedom and provide a better future for the next generation. **

My mom enrolled in public school and went on to become an elementary school librarian. My grandparents started a small business to recover some of what they had lost. In doing that, they gave me – the next generation – every opportunity America could offer.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106853/569209054/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk893-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTRkOWE2MC0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZzamQz8pvkRvwr6TbJLuiEb1WdY_bJ10H7xzAtY6o7E=)
From them and from my dad, who was a dedicated public servant,** I inherited the belief that we all have a responsibility to each ****other** – and to those who follow us. It is our job as Americans to extend opportunity, reward hard work and make sure everyone has a chance at the American dream.
These are the same lessons my wife, Susan, and I worked to pass on to our daughters. That's why, in the Senate, I fought to increase opportunity for Coloradans, lower costs and protect our public lands.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106854/569209055/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk893-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTRkOWE2MC0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZzamQz8pvkRvwr6TbJLuiEb1WdY_bJ10H7xzAtY6o7E=)
But Donald Trump doesn't believe in fighting for opportunity. Instead, he's taking a wrecking ball to our economy, and it's getting harder and harder to live in Colorado. **Now, my generation is the first one at risk of handing our children less opportunity – not more.**
At this point, I don't expect the solutions to the problems we're facing to come from Washington. They're going to have to come from us, right here in Colorado – including working to lower costs for housing, health care and child care, fighting corruption in government and building a better future for _all of us._
**[That's why I'm running for governor, and if you agree that our kids and grandkids deserve a governor who will fight for them, I hope you'll chip in to my campaign today. It's going to take all of us to win this race and create the future we deserve.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106855/569209056/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk893-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTRkOWE2MC0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZzamQz8pvkRvwr6TbJLuiEb1WdY_bJ10H7xzAtY6o7E=)**
| | [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106856/569209057/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk893-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTRkOWE2MC0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZzamQz8pvkRvwr6TbJLuiEb1WdY_bJ10H7xzAtY6o7E=)

I joined the California Democratic Party because I believe our democracy is worth fighting for, and I’ve jumped in at **one of the most critical moments in our state’s history** : the campaign to pass Proposition 50 this November 4. Donald Trump and Texas Republicans are trying to rig the 2026 elections before they even begin. Their dark money groups are flooding California with misinformation. And if we don’t fight back, Donald Trump will get two more years of a Congress that lets him run rampant. _That’s where my job comes in!_ I’m working ASAP to expand our organizing program — hiring canvassers, coordinating literature distribution, training volunteers, partnering with coalition leaders, and making sure every California voter has the facts about Prop 50. **But here’s the truth: I cannot do it alone.** So yes, I am going to ask for a donation. But as a lifelong organizer, I respect your time and your hard-earned money. I want you to know where your donation is going to go. _Every dollar you give will go directly into:_ 1️⃣ Printing pro-Prop 50 literature to counter GOP lies; 2️⃣ Funding canvasses in every corner of California; 3️⃣ Training the volunteer leaders who will help us turn out the vote; 4️⃣ Getting our voter protection resources (including our hotline) relaunched in time for the special election. **[Trump’s allies are counting on us to stumble in these first weeks. But if we scale up our organizing program now, we’ll be ready to meet their attacks head–on. That’s why I’m humbly asking: Will you make a special donation to our organizing efforts today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106933/569209308/-1861291874?&refcode=20250910_FR_45DO_OrganizingDirector&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2JlMGVhYi0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oUOqch7Ch4vKCuxx81FgQ6__Od5Yrl0lnYoobKLhNVQ=)** [CHIP IN $5 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115106934/569209309/-1861291874?&refcode=20250910_FR_45DO_OrganizingDirector&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2JlMGVhYi0wYjkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oUOqch7Ch4vKCuxx81FgQ6__Od5Yrl0lnYoobKLhNVQ=)

---
Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — **California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.**

clean_body: intro: | = | = Hi there, My name is Helen Amelga, and I’m the new Organizing and Training Director at the California Democratic Party. I joined the California Democratic Party because I believe our democracy is worth fighting for, and I’ve jumped in at one of the most critical moments in our state’s history : the campaign to pass Proposition 50 this November 4. Donald Trump and Texas Republicans are trying to rig the 2026 elections before they even begin. Their dark money groups are flooding California with misinformation. And if we don’t fight back, Donald Trump will get two more years of a Congress that lets him run rampant. That’s where my job comes in! I’m working ASAP to expand our organizing program — hiring canvassers, coordinating literature distribution, training volunteers, partnering with coalition leaders, and making sure every California voter has the facts about Prop 50. But here’s the truth: I cannot do it alone. So yes, I am going to ask for a donation. But as a lifelong organizer, I respect your time and your hard-earned money. I want you to know where your donation is going to go. Every dollar you give will go directly into: 1️⃣ Printing pro-Prop 50 literature to counter GOP lies; 2️⃣ Funding canvasses in every corner of California; 3️⃣ Training the volunteer leaders who will help us turn out the vote; 4️⃣ Getting our voter protection resources (including our hotline) relaunched in time for the special election. = = = = = = I am so honored to be on this team, and so thankful for your time! \- Helen P.S. Interested in getting involved with organizing? ! | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. If this email was forwarded to you, We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, And if you no longer wish to receive emails from the California Democratic Party, click here to . | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www.cadem.org | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. name: Chris Pappas
subject: BREAKING: GOP is recruiting John E. Sununu
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
This is urgent, Derek.
| | |
---
Derek, Chris Pappas here.
**National Republicans are recruiting John E. Sununu for U.S. Senate here in NH — and I need you to know what that means.**
Sununu represented New Hampshire in Congress nearly _twenty years ago_. And throughout his time in DC, he put special interests ahead of Granite Staters every chance he got.
**_His record speaks for itself_ — he voted against lowering drug prices, repeatedly voted to restrict abortion access, and led efforts to privatize Social Security. **
And what has he done since leaving office? Joined a lobbying firm that represented corporate special interests. Now he’s national Republicans’ handpicked candidate for this race. With name recognition like his, this is an all-hands-on-deck moment.
[**Please split $5 today between my campaign and John Fetterman's to stop national Republicans from buying this Senate seat.**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51649?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=1&akid=10301%2E434096%2EMV3C09)
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Chris Pappas and John Fetterman:_ | | | [DONATE $5 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51658?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=2&akid=10301%2E434096%2EMV3C09)

With gratitude,
Chris

| Public radio is a lifeline for Michiganders.

It delivers local news, emergency alerts, and educational programs like Sesame Street — reaching families in communities that are too often left behind by corporate media.

But now, because of Republican cuts, stations like Kalamaazoo’s WMUK re facing devastating funding losses. They’re being forced to make impossible choices: laying off staff, cutting programming, and scrambling to raise hundreds of thousands of dollars just to stay on the air.

I fought tooth and nail to protect this funding. But Republicans voted to rip it away. Now, our communities will pay the price.

[_**I’m calling on Congress to restore funding for public broadcasting immediately. Will you add your name to our petition today to show that Michiganders are united in demanding action to save public broadcasting?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115107252/569210269/-1526451433?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMjcwMzU0Mi0wYzkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XqDTj2ZCkc_YWYhlb6ES0KiLNu-y5_FloKFOgVZbAZU=&emci=e1703542-0c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e2703542-0c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=750857)

body:
We just received BIG news—your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California!

| _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/u/T2BWFyW25WuSA2laQqJLIzRqlVVHQqg_KnmTXBf3qLOSJ_2cvYw5xKm6h5xluZ9UqStFS9T79fjtUn8eFONCj4WIpopy-HYweJRn-K_FS_-lSaU55xmrfgMF8Bu7MiB0zrvafkNsDscjJqywjtf7EMaygJe0JZxSV_1US2npV2LItUuebeFZA5Eo/Zk1QTN9bFPejlC5kOOgBSWYkTK5QgMLf/12)_

**[Free to Meme—Court Says California Can’t Censor Political Jokes](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/NTKIBDXILJ9JP8fU8Et5CiswRKGGUfm6k4vRqCCY54EvNBg9C0zCeuq7Dat3T6F-Q7mLYFV0fp7oIdIPGBopHaf7JqminAD8XdEpJ9xA-wREsN769TJAllDL1-dbl41YS1_210DfmxiRolDf4pvgTyStJEVER-6dpie_S7FcjQczDBhLwpQzv2WRdg9Fdrcts-yK9UfmaEpWmXMF4uglnt8pZ_IiwjPYpQzjr8lZd6hZUcPZn1gBt5V0jnJGLNXWLjE32_dWu4zb28_0IqqDJWCv0I5OVCD-kaCKkgifx6Cl8C87JkUAQC_4Q_WtG9RKgO0NASyzkH_2Y4uoLjradmrdkijvL_UwNM8g2-ZV83uLo4x6EJVVvoxqtStHjR2rA6m5QKXjSlfM0LPzmsCm1zGiEnrgHVbAIL22WWEidFGL_7F_7dM34cIx_tpq5k1PpTJwMZxga3CSnerr8M1lObPsNVnZU6S5FrD7-5VuZF6Tcyl99OvJOSPPBOft1nCSCMlIHH81WnIph0WeePOpZVnlM_JkoxlkrZ_bT_nrzBjNZg21qiLqTWYC3WRimyP52uHKg5upsPH1cAjHvBJR4C7asHNmL4EhMwy3t6JyT-Y48Lz_FpNIyAOpk2IZiwBBAfeXq3xEIFSEbY_NFyrDSJtVgAcxn-6x4I3ocyoREWCj6vSWkgY9exD4LNTVKv50G_8Bup_I9Rt6O11fh-5T/bLXaIv0Bd5Es75uCjFw0q6YJ0JfoIsmc/12)** Hi Fellow American,

We just received BIG news--your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California!

The laws censor political speech and even punish people for posting a simple, funny meme and then require online platforms to censor that speech--if the government deems the content "materially deceptive." So, The Babylon Bee, along with a California lawyer and video-sharing platform Rumble, challenged the laws for violating their free speech rights--**and today, a court agreed with them.**

This is an incredible win for free speech. And it's because friends like YOU stood by The Babylon Bee and helped them fight this egregious government overreach.

But it's not the end of the story. The Babylon Bee's battle to protect humor and free speech isn't over.

Their rights--and yours--are still under threat in Hawaii, thanks to a law that goes even further than California's.

Hawaii's law threatens fines, damages, and even _jail time_ if a person posts a political meme that government officials don't like. It's all part of a growing trend of censorship. Across the country and the globe, government officials and private actors alike are cracking down on speech in cases like this.

With your help, The Babylon Bee is fighting Hawaii's law--no one should face jail time because a politician doesn't get your joke.

This victory in California is promising, but your help is needed to keep up the fight for free speech. clean_body: We just received BIG news—your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California! | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | Hi Fellow American, We just received BIG news--your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California! The laws censor political speech and even punish people for posting a simple, funny meme and then require online platforms to censor that speech--if the government deems the content "materially deceptive." So, The Babylon Bee, along with a California lawyer and video-sharing platform Rumble, challenged the laws for violating their free speech rights--and today, a court agreed with them. This is an incredible win for free speech. And it's because friends like YOU stood by The Babylon Bee and helped them fight this egregious government overreach. But it's not the end of the story. The Babylon Bee's battle to protect humor and free speech isn't over. Their rights--and yours--are still under threat in Hawaii, thanks to a law that goes even further than California's. Hawaii's law threatens fines, damages, and even jail time if a person posts a political meme that government officials don't like. It's all part of a growing trend of censorship. Across the country and the globe, government officials and private actors alike are cracking down on speech in cases like this. With your help, The Babylon Bee is fighting Hawaii's law--no one should face jail time because a politician doesn't get your joke. This victory in California is promising, but your help is needed to keep up the fight for free speech. For Freedom, Alliance Defending Freedom Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: First they tried to silence us. Then we got louder.
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250912-1519-foote&t=1001&akid=1519%2E342411%2EbA8j0M ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

FIRST: They tried to silence our voices.

From voter suppression and book bans to attacks on reproductive rights and
the criminalization of protest, authoritarian forces in this country are
rolling back hard-won freedoms and silencing the people who challenge
their power.

THEN: We built a movement.

Grassroots donors like you helped elect Ayanna, Alexandria, Ilhan, and
Rashida — bold, unapologetic progressives who are unafraid to speak truth
to power. And together, we’ve shown that politics doesn’t have to be
bought by billionaires — it can be powered by the people.

NOW: The stakes are higher than ever.

Authoritarianism isn’t just creeping in. It’s knocking at the door. Trump
and his MAGA allies are fast-tracking mass deportations, attacks on civil
liberties, and dismantling our democracy from within — just to name a few.

The Squad is on the front lines of resistance — but we can’t do it without
you.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250912-movement.W_52c9&refcode=em20250912-1519&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1519%2E342411%2EbA8j0M ]If you're with us in the fight against authoritarianism, chip in
$3 today to strengthen our movement.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250912-movement.W_52c9&refcode=em20250912-1519&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1519%2E342411%2EbA8j0M ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Mark Warner
subject: A story I don’t often tell:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
This is personal.

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115107593/569211341/1082117603?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Mom_250912_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NGQ5ZDYwNi0wZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3ZYK77BlSe-gkt8gwsO2B6H9SDhZ-Cs_O9Q9ZwjxrOs=) Derek,

As I travel across Virginia and talk to voters and constituents – there’s something that comes up over and over again:

Just about everyone is impacted in some way by Donald Trump’s gutting of Medicaid, the skyrocketing cost of health care, or the reckless cuts to our nation’s premier medical research.

**And let me tell you: It’s personal for me too.**

During the last years of my mother’s life, we watched her slow and difficult fight with Alzheimer’s disease. For 11 years she lived with the disease, and nine of those years, she couldn't speak. \------------------ Frankly, it’s something I don’t talk about often because it was just an awful disease and a difficult time for my family and me.

But it’s a reality far too many Americans know well – and it’s why I was disgusted and heartbroken to watch the DOGE bros freeze, delay and cut funding for lifesaving dementia research. **Lifesaving Alzheimer’s Research Delayed by Trump Funding Cuts:

_The Trump administration is freezing, delaying and revoking funding for dementia research, setting back discoveries of potential future treatment_

– Scientific American** How many years of progress did we lose by Donald Trump’s reckless administration?

How many more families will have to go through what my family went through?

And when will enough be enough for my Republican colleagues? When will they stand up to the stupid, harmful policies of the Trump administration? \------------------ Every day in the Senate, I am fighting back against these dangerous policies – introducing legislation to repeal the health care provisions in the ‘Big, Ugly Bill’ and permanently extend the Affordable Care Act’s tax credits; fighting to expand health care and benefits for Virginia’s nearly 700,000 veterans; and speaking out about the administration’s cuts to health and science research.

**And I’ve pushed my friends across the aisle to vote with their conscience and stand up to Trump’s reckless agenda. Unfortunately, too many of my Republican colleagues would rather cave to Trump than stand up for their constituents.**

It’s why we’ve got to take back the Senate next year. It’s why I’m fighting like hell to hold onto this seat for Democrats next year and doing all that I can to support Democrats in Virginia and in critical races across the country.

Thank you for being in this fight with us.

More soon,

Mark Warner **Before you go:**

Mark Warner is leading the fight to hold the Trump administration accountable – but that could make his race a top Republican target next year.

We need to be prepared for his toughest and most expensive reelection campaign yet.

[_**Will you consider a contribution today as an early investment in the campaign infrastructure we're building?
