clean_body: Schweikert may be giving up on AZ-01, but I’m not. | | = I need you to hear this from me: After 14 years of failing the hardworking folks of AZ-01, political pundits say that my GOP opponent, David Schweikert, is strongly weighing a run for governor. It should be no surprise that Schweikert is considering abandoning his district once again to pursue a higher office and align himself further with the GOP extremists that are running our country into the ground. I’m running for Congress to unseat Schweikert, fight for working families, and take on the Trump administration’s corruption — and yes, I’m actually in this race to serve AZ-01. = | = If you’ve been paying attention to Schweikert’s abysmal leadership, a run for higher office should be out of the question. ❌ He voted for the largest cut to Medicaid and SNAP benefits in American history. ❌ He continues to support an administration that has threatened critical benefits for our veterans. ❌ He has championed legislation that would put an end to reproductive freedom. Whether he runs for governor or stays in the race for AZ-01’s Congressional seat, one thing is clear: David Schweikert has no business representing Arizonans. = = | | = | = | = | = | = | = I’m ready to fight for our children, our seniors, our veterans, and all the communities Schweikert abandoned. Not backing down, Marlene | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. Chaparral Road, Suite A110-472 Scottsdale, AZ 85250 United States

name: Jennifer Jenkins
subject: Florida is winnable – so I’m running for US Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC
body:
I’m Jennifer Jenkins and today I’m announcing my Democratic Campaign for U.S. Senate in Florida to defeat Donald Trump and Ron DeSantis’s handpicked far-right candidate.
| [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4761?t=1&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
I’m Jennifer Jenkins and today I’m announcing my Democratic Campaign for U.S. Senate in Florida to defeat Donald Trump and Ron DeSantis’s handpicked far-right candidate.

And, if I’m being candid, I’m sick and tired of Democrats without a backbone who refuse to stand up to this administration.

**Florida is winnable. And right now, we have a golden opportunity to flip this Senate seat, and with it, the balance of power. If we succeed, Democrats can finally pass bold legislation to help American families like mine, and yours. If we fail, the GOP stays in charge and Donald Trump will have a rubber stamp for his dictatorship.**

[_**Can I count on you to split a $5 or $10 contribution between our campaign and The Long Run to power us from Week One and reach our $250,000 Launch Day Goal >>**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4762?t=2&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
| [DONATE ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4762?t=3&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
**I am a Democrat because I know what it feels like to struggle, to be left out,_to need someone in your corner._**

My dad worked at Rikers and multiple jobs to make ends meet. My mom fought like hell to get Social Security Disability benefits so she could survive her lifelong illness.

When the going got really tough, I delivered groceries to help make ends meet and keep my family afloat.

Let’s be real: Washington is broken. While MAGA Republicans push their extreme agenda – banning abortion, gutting voting rights, protecting billionaires, and turning a blind eye to gun violence – _**too many Democrats remain silent.**_

I’ve seen what happens when people back down from a fight. And I’m done waiting.

[_**Washington needs a fighter. Not another Trump lapdog. So please, join our movement from Week One and split a donation between my campaign and The Long Run right now to help me win in Florida >>**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4761?t=5&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
| [DONATE ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4761?t=6&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
Believe it or not, I ran against a MAGA lapdog before... __and won.__

When a MAGA extremist ran for school board in my kid’s district – I couldn’t sit back while teachers were being short-changed and our kids' futures were being put in jeopardy.

**I stood up. I ran for school board. and I won in a county where Democrats are outnumbered two-to-one. From day one, extremists tried to silence me. They protested outside my home, threatened me, and even cheered when the Governor singled me out as a target.**

But I refused to let my daughter see me give up while families across Florida needed someone to fight for them. I’m an educator. My husband is a teacher. Who was gonna fight for us?

[_**Me. That’s who. I refused to quit. That’s why I’m running now. Because Floridians deserve a battle-tested fighter in the U.S. Senate. And because Florida is winnable. I know a $5 split between my campaign and The Long Run may not seem like much. But if enough people chip in, we’ll hit our goal in no time and power this campaign to victory >>**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4762?t=8&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
| | [$3 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4763?t=9&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [$5 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4764?t=11&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [$10 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4765?t=13&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [$15 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4766?t=15&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [$25 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4767?t=17&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [$50 ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4768?t=19&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
| [OTHER AMOUNT ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4769?t=21&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---
I can’t win this without you.

Donald Trump and Ron DeSantis will spend whatever it takes to defeat me. I’m counting on grassroots donors like you chipping in to emails like this one to power this campaign.

[_**So please, join our movement from Week One and chip in right now between my campaign and The Long Run to help me win in Florida >>**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4761?t=23&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)

Let’s do this,

Jennifer Jenkins
* * *
| | [ ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4775?t=24&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)

---

P.O. Box 597, Milwaukee, WI 53201
| [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4144?t=25&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX) | [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4145?t=26&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX) | | [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4146?t=27&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---|---|---|---
[CONTRIBUTE TODAY](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4775?t=28&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)
---

We'd hate to see you go, but if you'd like, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=30&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX). You can also sign up to [receive fewer emails](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=31&akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX).
This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.mandelabarnes.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=5604%2E196590%2EVY6CZX)

subject: January 6 Prosecutions Update!
body:
FEMA Employees Caught Watching Porn at Work

[INSIDE JW]

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES JUSTICE DEPARTMENT OVER LAST-MINUTE JANUARY 6
TARGETING

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36068&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1uMEFxVnljcUhIRSZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=fc9502f764addf34d2edbb88be9c92013608d326a44df1745a3420b867d63300]

The Biden administration, anticipating President-elect Donald
Trump’s promise to issue pardons for January 6, 2021, defendants, is
believed to have accelerated prosecutions in the final months of
Biden’s term.

That is why we filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36055&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLXRydW1wLXBhcmRvbnMtY29tcGxhaW50LTAyMTYxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b82acb3cc9c76bd8cd04e086d5e7cc4fb9c55539f96b98736815ffbc932c6e60]
against the U.S. Department of Justice for records on accelerated
January 6 prosecutions after Donald Trump was elected president in
November 2024 (_Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36055&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLXRydW1wLXBhcmRvbnMtY29tcGxhaW50LTAyMTYxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b82acb3cc9c76bd8cd04e086d5e7cc4fb9c55539f96b98736815ffbc932c6e60]
(No. 1:25-cv-02161)).

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the Justice Department failed to respond to a November 2024 FOIA
request for the records of then-Attorney General Merrick Garland,
then-Deputy Attorney General Lisa O. Monaco, then-Principal Deputy
Associate Attorney General Benjamin C. Mizer, and then-Special Counsel
Jack Smith “regarding a) pardons of any person(s) incarcerated due
convictions in federal court in the District of Columbia related to
crimes committed at the U.S. Capitol in Washington, D.C, on Wednesday,
Jan. 6, 2021 b) directives or orders to focus on arresting additional
suspects by type of offense allegedly committed. The timeframe of the
request is from January – November 2024.”

On January 6, 2025, the Justice Department reportedly
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36056&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm94bmV3cy5jb20vcG9saXRpY3MvZG9qLWNvbnNpZGVycy1jaGFyZ2luZy0yMDAtbW9yZS1wZW9wbGUtNC15ZWFycy1hZnRlci1qYW4tNi1jYXBpdG9sLWF0dGFjay5hbXA/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ec07d4bffb1cfd4c3a887620e031d679e7f0cb7e46e3a87a37a837ae535347a7]
considered charging up to 200 additional people
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36057&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucG9saXRpY28uY29tL25ld3MvMjAyNS8wMS8wNi9qYW51YXJ5LTYtY2FzZXMtZG9uYWxkLXRydW1wLTAwMTk2NTAxP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=eb5a3c1e603a9029cfb1d7891b29dce27f6af25b65157216a58d5785b10aa6bc]
for their alleged involvement at the U.S. Capitol disturbance. About
1,583 people already had been facing federal charges. On January 17,
2025, it was reported that the FBI and federal prosecutors continued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36058&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnRpbWVzLmNvbS9uZXdzLzIwMjUvamFuLzE3L2JpZGVucy1kb2otcnVzaGVzLWZpbGUtamFuLTYtY2FzZXMtYWhlYWQtdHJ1Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=466dc432da8e41342f32a791bbf895c3deeae4d5ff3557bd4151f85d7a09bbe3]
to make arrests and “pursue hefty sentences for those who breached
the U.S. Capitol grounds.” Authorities made four arrests just days
before Trump’s inauguration.

The corrupt Biden Justice Department tried to undermine the Trump
administration by maliciously prosecuting American citizens tied to
January 6. Our lawsuit aims to get to the truth about this last-minute
abuse of power.

We have several FOIA lawsuits dealing with the lawfare targeting Trump
and his supporters.

In August 2025, we announced that the U.S. Air Force will finally
provide
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36059&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZnVuZXJhbC1ob25vcnMtYXNobGktYmFiYml0dC9dZnVsbD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=7707e318ec9012150ce4f91e6ade71673001ea183e930193a65609f208b18102
military funeral honors
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=35989&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZnVuZXJhbC1ob25vcnMtYXNobGktYmFiYml0dC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=238d6da3fc522617b83f0f5ba9a1a2f959fb37351b5cd24d9e71224ef4f9a75c]
to Ashli
Babbitt, the Air Force veteran who was shot and killed inside the U.S.
Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6, 2021.
Babbitt was the only official January 6 homicide victim. The Biden
administration had previously denied Babbitt and her family these
honors in retaliation for being at the U.S. Capitol that day. This
decision comes on the heels of a massive, nearly $5 million Trump
administration
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36060&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvNC05NzUtbWlsbGlvbi1zZXR0bGVtZW50LW9mLWFzaGxpLWJhYmJpdHQvXXNldHRsZW1lbnQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e934dbfbef915bb64ebe891a0a8487532fdda422c23745bc00ddade4c2dd557f
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=35379&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvNC05NzUtbWlsbGlvbi1zZXR0bGVtZW50LW9mLWFzaGxpLWJhYmJpdHQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=1156af566a79604da3473b2dff32085037134316fdf9b21e745c7773c7f59cce]
to her family for wrongful death and other claims against the U.S.
Government.

Also in August, a Georgia state court issued an
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36061&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZmFuaS13aWxsaXMtdHJ1bXAtcmVjb3Jkcy9db3JkZXI/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=022f7500d4f3c4b454984b33f3e46b1dc38be79c750c8e5afb4d95bfda77c442
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=35957&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZmFuaS13aWxsaXMtdHJ1bXAtcmVjb3Jkcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5c5031b6fcfc0ff4dfd7f090d0ab70214dd8bf20c74bcea5ed455c27622d43c3]
in our
lawsuit for Georgia District Attorney Fani Willis to provide new
information and potentially conduct a new search for Trump-related
records because her affidavit to the court made no reference to
whether any searches of the devices of former Fulton County Special
Prosecutor Nathan Wade or those of Chief Investigator Michael L. Hill,
who was involved in gathering evidence and coordinating investigative
efforts and likely met
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=35952&F=T&&hash=9fbc743da6fc9d687b6929f69b9e729419b47b40896a679a6760792a426de17b]
with the January 6 Committee.

In May 2025, we
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36063&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ2FybGFuZC1taWxsZXktbWVldGluZy1hYm91dC10cnVtcC9dc3VlZD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=68d65a09db0f29f95be329284f9a6825e2d172e1f05cc63ba332e734052434bf
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=35108&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ2FybGFuZC1taWxsZXktbWVldGluZy1hYm91dC10cnVtcC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4fa6675e27b42627a0524e0d1a91f3280365171a2bdc7d71d2354b215406d57a]
the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding a reported 2021
meeting between then-Attorney General Merrick Garland and
then-Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Mark Milley during
which they discussed President Trump. During the meeting, Milley is

FEMA, LATEST AGENCY WITH EMPLOYEES WATCHING PORN AT WORK

Most of us believe that Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)
employees are dedicated to keeping us safe, but it turns out many of
them were watching porn at work on government computers. Our
_Corruption Chronicles_ blog reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36069&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZW1wbG95ZWVzLXdhdGNoaW5nLXBvcm4tZHVyaW5nLXdvcmsvXTo/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9a4c35526cc4a879ae65cb2e680465c46b5358137b835e193d91266c7b2d968b

> Over a decade after legislation was introduced in Congress to
> contain an epidemic of federal workers watching pornography on
> government computers, the nation’s Homeland Security Secretary has
> fired numerous employees “for consuming pornographic content
> during work hours while on government issued devices,” according
> to statement
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36070&F=T&&hash=413b7e3109a3d428324b3380a891d90355cb95c25beac7397d7f573553cd9fba]
> issued a few days ago by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
> “Today, I am announcing that multiple disgraced FEMA [Federal
> Emergency Management Agency] employees were fired for using their
> government devices to consume sexually explicit content while on the
> job,” said DHS Secretary Kristi Noem in the recent announcement.
> “These individuals had access to critical information and
> intelligence and were entrusted to safeguard Americans from
> emergencies—and instead they were consuming pornography.” The
> DHS secretary added that “in at least one case the pornography
> consumed was racially charged and involved bestiality.”

> During the recent audit, DHS investigators also discovered that
> nearly half of the FEMA staff regularly log into social media
> platforms while performing taxpayer funded duties. DHS’s Insider
> Threat Operations Center (ITOC) identified that one employee typed
> explicit and sexually charged phrases into a chatbot website from
> his government devices for the purpose of having the comments read
> back to him in an accent. Another individual, a government
> contracted employee also stationed at the command center, accessed a
> popular social media platform 578 times over a 30-day period. He was
> engaged in extensive interactions with individuals online, viewing
> explicit sexual content while on his work devices. He also accessed
> content that included graphic photographs and videos and, on several
> occasions, used his government computer to chat online with members
> presenting explicit content. The examples appear to be a snippet of
> the porn problem at FEMA, the beleaguered agency created by Jimmy
> Carter to help Americans recover from disasters.

> Porn has for years been part of the job at some government agencies
> and in fact, numerous federal audits have long documented the
> enraging details of public servants consuming it during business
> hours. Judicial Watch has reported on the scandal for over 15 years,
> beginning with the porn scandal at the Securities and Exchange
> Commission
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36071&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc2VjLWFic29yYmVkLXBvcm4tZHVyaW5nLWVjb25vbWljLWNyaXNpcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=7f7cc0271ca571f517b687db7a39bf42c685aa8d0b2a7cbaff329a6e26483d4a]
> (SEC), the agency charged with policing the nation’s financial
> industry. While the economy crumbled, the SEC was preoccupied with
> pornography. In fact, high-ranking managers at the agency regularly
> spent work hours gawking at pornography web sites on their
> government computers while the country’s financial system
> collapsed. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) has also been
> embroiled in a large-scale pornography scandal with the agency’s
> inspector general issuing a special alert involving a “significant
> increase
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36072&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc2lnbmlmaWNhbnQtaW5jcmVhc2UtdXNkYS1lbXBsb3llZXMtdmlld2luZy1wb3JuLWdvdnQtY29tcHV0ZXJzLXdvcmsvXeKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=aa6261acea990617bd42702d9f43bdd2ce328d2137d62f7cf8737edfd0351694
> in the number of employees and contractors viewing and sharing
> unlawful or otherwise inappropriate pornographic content—including
> child pornography—using their government-issued computers and
> other communication devices. The National Science Foundation (NSF)
> has also been exposed by its inspector general for employees
> spending significant portions of their workdays watching,
> downloading and e-mailing pornography
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=36073&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ292dC13b3JrZXJzLXN1cmYtcG9ybi1qb2IvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=0b9343ab9ec27e4be00033f6081d78b1cfdee073a6d250c7343db55c0b808bba]
on
> government computers without ever getting caught. The Environmental
> Protection Agency’s inspector general has also uncovered multiple
> cases of employees working hard at watching porn.

> Government employees viewing porn on taxpayer dime has been so
> pervasive that over ten years ago a bill, Eliminating Pornography
> from Agencies Act
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=76573&L=4347&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY29uZ3Jlc3MuZ292L2JpbGwvMTEzdGgtY29uZ3Jlc3MvaG91c2UtYmlsbC81NjI4P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=b769523b5d532a51031c1a96c2c68d1fee1c48724ddcb7f60f93729c4950064c],
was
> introduced in Congress to contain the embarrassing crisis. Though it
> was never enacted, the proposed law brought much-needed attention to
> the matter and inspired some agencies to address the problem
> individually. The North Carolina congressman who introduced the
> measure over a decade ago said it was necessary to stop federal
> employees from accessing, watching or sharing pornography on
> government-issued computers and devices. The former Republican
> lawmaker introduced the bill after the EPA scandal, which included
> one veteran employee viewing as much as six hours of pornography a
> day in his office, according to a federal audit. The same employee
> downloaded thousands of pornographic files onto his government
> computer, and another used his official EPA email to access an
> internet site with child pornography. The former congressman, Mark
> Meadows, who introduced the anti-porn bill, rightfully said it was
> appalling that it requires an act of Congress to ensure that federal
> agencies block access to porn sites.

Until next week,

clean_body: FEMA Employees Caught Watching Porn at Work [INSIDE JW] JUDICIAL WATCH SUES JUSTICE DEPARTMENT OVER LAST-MINUTE JANUARY 6 TARGETING [] The Biden administration, anticipating President-elect Donald Trump’s promise to issue pardons for January 6, 2021, defendants, is believed to have accelerated prosecutions in the final months of Biden’s term. That is why we filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the U.S. Department of Justice for records on accelerated January 6 prosecutions after Donald Trump was elected president in November 2024 (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:25-cv-02161)). We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after the Justice Department failed to respond to a November 2024 FOIA request for the records of then-Attorney General Merrick Garland, then-Deputy Attorney General Lisa O. Monaco, then-Principal Deputy Associate Attorney General Benjamin C. Mizer, and then-Special Counsel Jack Smith “regarding a) pardons of any person(s) incarcerated due convictions in federal court in the District of Columbia related to crimes committed at the U.S. Capitol in Washington, D.C, on Wednesday, Jan. 6, 2021 b) directives or orders to focus on arresting additional suspects by type of offense allegedly committed. The timeframe of the request is from January – November 2024.” On January 6, 2025, the Justice Department reportedly [] considered charging up to 200 additional people [] for their alleged involvement at the U.S. Capitol disturbance. About 1,583 people already had been facing federal charges. On January 17, 2025, it was reported that the FBI and federal prosecutors continued [] to make arrests and “pursue hefty sentences for those who breached the U.S. Capitol grounds.” Authorities made four arrests just days before Trump’s inauguration. The corrupt Biden Justice Department tried to undermine the Trump administration by maliciously prosecuting American citizens tied to January 6. Our lawsuit aims to get to the truth about this last-minute abuse of power. We have several FOIA lawsuits dealing with the lawfare targeting Trump and his supporters. In August 2025, we announced that the U.S. Air Force will finally provide [ military funeral honors [] to Ashli Babbitt, the Air Force veteran who was shot and killed inside the U.S. Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6, 2021. Babbitt was the only official January 6 homicide victim. The Biden administration had previously denied Babbitt and her family these honors in retaliation for being at the U.S. Capitol that day. This decision comes on the heels of a massive, nearly $5 million Trump administration [ [] to her family for wrongful death and other claims against the U.S. Government. Also in August, a Georgia state court issued an [ [] in our lawsuit for Georgia District Attorney Fani Willis to provide new information and potentially conduct a new search for Trump-related records because her affidavit to the court made no reference to whether any searches of the devices of former Fulton County Special Prosecutor Nathan Wade or those of Chief Investigator Michael L. Hill, who was involved in gathering evidence and coordinating investigative efforts and likely met [] with the January 6 Committee. In May 2025, we [ [] the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding a reported 2021 meeting between then-Attorney General Merrick Garland and then-Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Mark Milley during which they discussed President Trump. During the meeting, Milley is alleged to have pressured Garland to target “far-right” militia movements (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Defense [ (No. 1:25-cv-01330)). In April 2025, we [ [] a FOIA lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service to obtain records related to possible improper targeting of January 6, 2021, Capitol protesters, their supporters, and related nonprofits (Judicial Watch v. Internal Revenue Service [] (No. 1:25-cv-01290)). In February 2025, we [ [] the U.S. Department of Defense for information about two conference calls involving then-Chairman of the Joint Chief of Staff Mark Milley with the Departments of Justice, Interior and Homeland Security regarding coordination for the January 6, 2021, election certification (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Defense [] (No. 1:25-cv-00439)). In December 2024, we [ [] the Justice Department for details of a reported meeting between Attorney General Merrick Garland and. Milley in which they discussed President Trump and during which General Milley pressured Garland to target American “far right” militia movements (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:24-cv-03380)). FEMA, LATEST AGENCY WITH EMPLOYEES WATCHING PORN AT WORK Most of us believe that Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) employees are dedicated to keeping us safe, but it turns out many of them were watching porn at work on government computers. Our Corruption Chronicles_ blog reports [ Over a decade after legislation was introduced in Congress to contain an epidemic of federal workers watching pornography on government computers, the nation’s Homeland Security Secretary has fired numerous employees “for consuming pornographic content during work hours while on government issued devices,” according to statement [] issued a few days ago by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). “Today, I am announcing that multiple disgraced FEMA [Federal Emergency Management Agency] employees were fired for using their government devices to consume sexually explicit content while on the job,” said DHS Secretary Kristi Noem in the recent announcement. “These individuals had access to critical information and intelligence and were entrusted to safeguard Americans from emergencies—and instead they were consuming pornography.” The DHS secretary added that “in at least one case the pornography consumed was racially charged and involved bestiality.” During the recent audit, DHS investigators also discovered that nearly half of the FEMA staff regularly log into social media platforms while performing taxpayer funded duties. DHS’s Insider Threat Operations Center (ITOC) identified that one employee typed explicit and sexually charged phrases into a chatbot website from his government devices for the purpose of having the comments read back to him in an accent. Another individual, a government contracted employee also stationed at the command center, accessed a popular social media platform 578 times over a 30-day period. He was engaged in extensive interactions with individuals online, viewing explicit sexual content while on his work devices. He also accessed content that included graphic photographs and videos and, on several occasions, used his government computer to chat online with members presenting explicit content. The examples appear to be a snippet of the porn problem at FEMA, the beleaguered agency created by Jimmy Carter to help Americans recover from disasters. Porn has for years been part of the job at some government agencies and in fact, numerous federal audits have long documented the enraging details of public servants consuming it during business hours. Judicial Watch has reported on the scandal for over 15 years, beginning with the porn scandal at the Securities and Exchange Commission [] (SEC), the agency charged with policing the nation’s financial industry. While the economy crumbled, the SEC was preoccupied with pornography. In fact, high-ranking managers at the agency regularly spent work hours gawking at pornography web sites on their government computers while the country’s financial system collapsed. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) has also been embroiled in a large-scale pornography scandal with the agency’s inspector general issuing a special alert involving a “significant increase [ in the number of employees and contractors viewing and sharing unlawful or otherwise inappropriate pornographic content—including child pornography—using their government-issued computers and other communication devices. The National Science Foundation (NSF) has also been exposed by its inspector general for employees spending significant portions of their workdays watching, downloading and e-mailing pornography [] on government computers without ever getting caught. The Environmental Protection Agency’s inspector general has also uncovered multiple cases of employees working hard at watching porn. Government employees viewing porn on taxpayer dime has been so pervasive that over ten years ago a bill, Eliminating Pornography from Agencies Act [], was introduced in Congress to contain the embarrassing crisis. Though it was never enacted, the proposed law brought much-needed attention to the matter and inspired some agencies to address the problem individually. The North Carolina congressman who introduced the measure over a decade ago said it was necessary to stop federal employees from accessing, watching or sharing pornography on government-issued computers and devices. The former Republican lawmaker introduced the bill after the EPA scandal, which included one veteran employee viewing as much as six hours of pornography a day in his office, according to a federal audit. The same employee downloaded thousands of pornographic files onto his government computer, and another used his official EPA email to access an internet site with child pornography. The former congressman, Mark Meadows, who introduced the anti-porn bill, rightfully said it was appalling that it requires an act of Congress to ensure that federal agencies block access to porn sites. Until next week, [Contribute] [] [advertisement] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2025, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

[__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115116927/569241688/942454713?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjY4MmM5OC0yZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j77z1Kt7QhJRQicv_Lhm-jeuDCLCTjlDplvLPh0peUQ=&emci=c5e2a4f7-2c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=eb682c98-2e90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=4925535) On Thursday, September 18 at 1 PM ET, our party is hosting a rally at the Indiana Statehouse with a special guest to be announced! This is our moment to get loud, Hoosiers! Republicans want to cheat their way to total control over all of our Congressional seats, despite the fact that Democrats regularly register over 40% of the Statewide vote. They know they are losing on the issues nationally, and they want to rig the system against regular people at every step. They know that without the cheating, they will lose badly in 2026. Even if they draw new maps, we’re prepared to make it backfire on them. **[Sign up today to join us at the Statehouse.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115116928/569241689/1230792341?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjY4MmM5OC0yZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j77z1Kt7QhJRQicv_Lhm-jeuDCLCTjlDplvLPh0peUQ=&emci=c5e2a4f7-2c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=eb682c98-2e90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=4925535)** ****_Republicans Vote Again Against Releasing Epstein Files in Committee_
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115116929/569241690/972764065?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjY4MmM5OC0yZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j77z1Kt7QhJRQicv_Lhm-jeuDCLCTjlDplvLPh0peUQ=&emci=c5e2a4f7-2c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=eb682c98-2e90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=4925535) Another week has gone by without a single Indiana Republican coming out in favor of releasing the Epstein Files. The bipartisan discharge petition still has the signatures of just four House Republicans nationally, with none of them being from Indiana. On top of that, Rep. Erin Houchin missed a Rules Committee meeting this week where a motion to force a vote on their release was tabled by the Republican majority. It is yet another sign that Republicans are only looking to hide the facts from Hoosiers and all Americans. It makes us wonder: who are they trying to protect? ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115116930/569241691/289507754?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjY4MmM5OC0yZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j77z1Kt7QhJRQicv_Lhm-jeuDCLCTjlDplvLPh0peUQ=&emci=c5e2a4f7-2c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=eb682c98-2e90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=4925535) [Share Indiana House Democrats](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115116931/569241692/289507754?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjY4MmM5OC0yZTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j77z1Kt7QhJRQicv_Lhm-jeuDCLCTjlDplvLPh0peUQ=&emci=c5e2a4f7-2c90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=eb682c98-2e90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=4925535) After the tragic assassination of conservative commentator Charlie Kirk this week, State Rep. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** INDems to Hold Statehouse Rally Against Gerrymandering With Special Guest on September 18 at 1 PM ET On Thursday, September 18 at 1 PM ET, our party is hosting a rally at the Indiana Statehouse with a special guest to be announced! This is our moment to get loud, Hoosiers! Republicans want to cheat their way to total control over all of our Congressional seats, despite the fact that Democrats regularly register over 40% of the Statewide vote. They know they are losing on the issues nationally, and they want to rig the system against regular people at every step. They know that without the cheating, they will lose badly in 2026. Even if they draw new maps, we’re prepared to make it backfire on them. Republicans Vote Again Against Releasing Epstein Files in Committee Another week has gone by without a single Indiana Republican coming out in favor of releasing the Epstein Files. The bipartisan discharge petition still has the signatures of just four House Republicans nationally, with none of them being from Indiana. On top of that, Rep. Erin Houchin missed a Rules Committee meeting this week where a motion to force a vote on their release was tabled by the Republican majority. It is yet another sign that Republicans are only looking to hide the facts from Hoosiers and all Americans. It makes us wonder: who are they trying to protect? Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. After the tragic assassination of conservative commentator Charlie Kirk this week, State Rep. Victoria Garcia Wilburn joined Republican State Rep. Beau Baird in condemning the murder and calling for an immediate return to civility, and end to political violence in our country. We were heartbroken by the tragic events that took place this week, and we condemn all forms of political violence. No one should feel unsafe in America for expressing their first amendment rights. Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Make Your Voice Heard! Submit Public Input on Upcoming Indiana Projects INDOT – INDOT is taking comments on its proposed upgrades and changes to the I-80/94 corridor in Northwest Indiana (FlexRoad), which is the busiest stretch of interstate in Indiana. Bartholomew Co – The Indiana Office of Utility Consumer Counselor (OUCC) is asking for public comments on Southwestern Bartholomew Water Corporation’s rate increase request, Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Wednesday, September 17th from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on How Local Parties Prepare for GOTV. INDems IN the News IndyStar: WTHR: Indiana Capital Chronicle: * Indiana Capital Chronicle: [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

TEARS OF JOY!! [ **PART TWO:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=5&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) Adam Schiff introduced a STUNNING Constitutional Amendment to OVERTURN _Citizens United_ once and for all! []() [ **PART THREE:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=6&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) EVERYONE agrees that _Citizens United_ has to be overturned. That’s why Adam Schiff and Top Democrats are doing everything they can to pass a Constitutional Amendment! [ **_But we NEED 100,000 signatures on our petition ASAP to see this through. We CAN overturn_ Citizens United _by passing our Constitutional Amendment and SAVE our democracy,[but we need your help NOW:_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=8&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) | [SIGN HERE: PASS THE CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENT TO OVERTURN _CITIZENS UNITED_ >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=9&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ)
---

Nothing, and we mean NOTHING, would make Mitch McConnell more enraged than to see our top supporters rallying behind Adam Schiff!

He LOVES that the _Citizens United_ decision allows his Dark Money buddies to FLOOD every race with BOATLOADS of cash to steamroll Democrats.

[ **HARD TRUTH:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=11&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) If Democrats EVER want a chance at ENDING corruption in our democracy, we need to send _Citizens United_ into the ash heap of history.

But we can’t pass the Amendment without you. We want to be clear: [**_Your signature is required in order for us to stand a chance._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=12&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) [ **Please do the right thing and sign now:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=13&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ) [SIGN HERE: OVERTURN _CITIZENS UNITED_ >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68751?t=14&akid=23387%2E4449941%2EYoUrzJ)

Peter ,
National Republicans just jumped headfirst into New Jersey's governor's race with a super PAC backed by the Republican Governors Association that's dropping a $1 million ad blitz to boost Trump's handpicked candidate, Jack Ciattarelli.
They think New Jersey is ripe for a MAGA takeover, and with Donald Trump himself backing Ciattarelli, the GOP is desperate to flip this state and prove their message of extremism and division is winning.
If we want to keep New Jersey blue and protect progress on issues like affordable energy, reproductive rights, and democracy itself, we need to fight back starting right now.
That's why GOTV PAC is getting ready to launch our winning phone and text banks to cut through GOP smears and make sure turnout is strong enough to beat back this national Republican assault. But we can't do it without you.
Will you rush a donation today to help us fight back against this $1 million Republican ad buy? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09122025]
